aim.definition: The ability of someone to aim straight; one's faculty for being able to hit a physical target

aim.en: He aimed at the target, but the arrow flew straight over it.

To direct the intention or purpose; to attempt the accomplishment of a purpose; to try to gain; to endeavor;—followed by at, or by an infinitive

aim.en: She aimed a punch at her ex-boyfriend.
aim.definition: To direct or point (e.g. a weapon), at a particular object; to direct, as a missile, an act, or a proceeding, at, to, or against an object

aim.en: to aim a satirical comment at Communists in general
aim.definition: To direct (something verbal) towards a certain person, thing, or group

air.en: I'm going outside to get some air.
air.definition: The substance constituting earth's atmosphere, particularly:

air.en: The flock of birds took to the air.
air.definition: (usually with the) The apparently open space above the ground which this substance fills, formerly thought to be limited by the firmament but now considered to be surrounded by the near vacuum of outer space.

air.en: to give it an air of artistry and sophistication

air.definition: (usually in the plural) Pretension; snobbishness; pretence that one is better than others.

air.en: Could you turn on the air?
air.definition: An air conditioner or the processed air it produces.

air.en: This game show first aired in the 1990s and is still going today.

airport.definition: An airfield (an area designated for the takeoff and landing of aircraft), including one or more runways and (for commercial airports) one or more passenger terminals.

alarm.en: The clock radio is a friendlier version of the cold alarm by the bedside.
alarm.definition: A mechanical device for awaking people, or rousing their attention.

alarm.en: You should set the alarm on your watch to go off at seven o'clock.
alarm.definition: An instance of an alarm ringing, beeping or clanging, to give a noise signal at a certain time.

alive.en: As long as the plant is alive, he will continue to water it.

alive.definition: In a state of action; in force or operation; existent

alive.en: The post office will not ship live animals.

alive.en: He is a live example of the consequences of excessive drinking.

alive.definition: Seen or heard from a broadcast, as it happens.

alive.definition: (of a performance or speech) In person.
(entertainment, performing) Recorded in front of an audience and not having been edited after recording.

alive.en: The air force practices dropping live bombs on the uninhabited island.
alive.definition: Of firearms or explosives, capable of causing harm.

alive.en: Use caution when working near live wires.
alive.definition: (circuitry) Electrically charged or energized, usually indicating that the item may cause electrocution if touched.

alive.definition: Being a bet which can be raised by the bettor, usually in reference to a blind or straddle.

all.en: She gave her all, and collapsed at the finish line.
all.definition: (with a possessive pronoun) Everything that one is capable of.

all.en: The score was 30 all when the rain delay started.

A good time was had by all.
All that was left was a small pile of ash.

allow.en: to allow a servant his liberty;  to allow a free passage;  to allow one day for rest
allow.definition: To grant, give, admit, accord, afford, or yield; to let one have.

allow.en: to allow a right;  to allow a claim;  to allow the truth of a proposition
allow.definition: To acknowledge; to accept as true; to concede; to accede to an opinion.

almost.en: In all the submissions, they found four papers that were clearly worth publishing and another dozen almosts.
almost.definition: Something or someone that doesn't quite make it.

almost.en: Almost all people went there. - Not all but very close to it.

alone.en: I can't ask for help because I am alone.

alone.definition: Apart from, or exclusive of, others.
By oneself; apart from, or exclusive of, others; solo.

alone.en: The job was too hard for me to do alone.

along.en: John played the piano and everyone sang along.
Don't stop here. Just move along.

already.definition: An intensifier used to emphasize impatience or express exasperation.

also.en: They had porridge for breakfast, and also toast.

alternative.definition: Relating to a choice between two or more possibilities.

although.en: Although it was very muddy, the football game went on.

altogether.definition: On the whole; with everything considered.

always.en: Airplanes did not always exist as a form of transportation.

ambition.en: Pausanias, ambitioning the sovereignty of Greece, bargains with Xerxes for his daughter in marriage. — Trumbull.
ambition.definition: To seek after ambitiously or eagerly; to covet.

among.en: How can you speak with authority about their customs when you have never lived among them?

amount.en: The amount of atmospheric pollution threatens a health crisis.

amount.en: He was a pretty good student, but never amounted to much professionally.

amuse.en: I watch these movies because they amuse me.
amuse.definition: To entertain or occupy in a pleasant manner; to stir with pleasing emotions.

amuse.en: His jokes rarely fail to amuse.
amuse.definition: To cause laughter or amusement; to be funny.

ancient.en: an ancient city  an ancient forest
ancient.definition: Having lasted from a remote period; having been of long duration; of great age, very old.

ancient.en: an ancient author  an ancient empire
ancient.definition: Existent or occurring in time long past, usually in remote ages; belonging to or associated with antiquity; old, as opposed to modern.

anger.en: You need to control your anger.

anger.en: He who angers you conquers you.

angle.en: the angle between lines A and B
angle.definition: A figure formed by two rays which start from a common point (a plane angle) or by three planes that intersect (a solid angle).

angle.en: The angle between lines A and B is π/4 radians, or 45 degrees.
angle.definition: The measure of such a figure. In the case of a plane angle, this is the ratio (or proportional to the ratio) of the arc length to the radius of a section of a circle cut by the two rays, centered at their common point. In the case of a solid angle, this is the ratio of the surface area to the square of the radius of the section of a sphere.
A corner where two walls intersect.

angle.en: The horse took off at an angle.
His angle is that he gets a percentage, but mostly in trade.

angle.definition: To present or argue something in a particular way or from a particular viewpoint.

angry.en: An angry mob started looting the warehouse.

angry.definition: (said about the elements, like the sky or the sea) Dark and stormy, menacing.

animal.definition: In scientific usage, a multicellular organism that is usually mobile, whose cells are not encased in a rigid cell wall (distinguishing it from plants and fungi) and which derives energy solely from the consumption of other organisms (distinguishing it from plants).

A person of a particular type.

annual.definition: An annual publication; a book, periodical, journal, report, comic book, yearbook, etc., which is published serially once a year, which may or may not be in addition to regular weekly or monthly publication.

annual.definition: An annual plant; a plant with a life span of just one growing season; a plant which naturally germinates, flowers and dies in one year. Compare biennial, perennial.

annual.en: an annual general meeting;  an annual publication
an annual salary;  average annual profits;  the annual course of the sun
annual.definition: Of, for, or relating to a whole year, often as a recurring cycle; determined or reckoned by the year; accumulating in the course of a year; performed, executed, or completed over the course of a year. See also circannual.

answer.en: He answered my claim upon him.
answer.definition: To be or act in compliance with, in fulfillment or satisfaction of, as an order, obligation, or demand.

arm.definition: A long, narrow, more or less rigid part of an object extending from the main part or centre of the object, such as the arm of an armchair, a crane, a pair of spectacles or a pair of compasses.

arrange.en: to arrange to meet;   to arrange for supper

arrival.en: The early arrival of the bride created a stir.

art.en: She's mastered the art of programming.

art.en: He's at university to study art.

article.definition: An object, a member of a group or class.

article.en: The Articles of War are a set of regulations [...] to govern the conduct of [...] military [...] forces

article.en: Each of the chelicerae is composed of two articles, forming a powerful pincer.

artificial.en: The flowers were artificial, and he thought them rather tacky.

as.en: 1865, The Act of Suicide as Distinct from the Crime of Self-Murder: A Sermon

Introducing a basis of comparison, with an object in the objective case.

as.definition: In the (same) way or manner that; to the (same) degree that.

Note that in an ordinary context, when talking about a bag of apples, one does not care about identifying the individual apples, although one might be interested in distinguishing apples by species, for example, letting 'r' denote 'red apple' and 'g' denote 'green apple', then a bag of three red apples and two green apples could be denoted as {r,r,r,g,g}.

bar.definition: Denotes the minimum odds offered on other horses not mentioned by name.

bargain.en: At that price, it's not just a bargain, it's a steal.
bargain.definition: A gainful transaction; an advantageous purchase.

bargain.en: They had to bargain for a few minutes to get a decent price for the rug.
bargain.definition: To make a bargain; to make a deal or contract for the exchange of property or services; to negotiate

base.en: The logarithm to base 2 of 8 is 3.
base.definition: A number raised to the power of an exponent.

base.en: Peter adjusted the equalizer on his audio equipment to emphasize the bass.
base.definition: A low spectrum of sound tones.

base.en: The conductor preferred to situate the bass in the middle rear, rather than to one side of the orchestra.
base.definition: A section of musical group that produces low-pitched sound, lower than the baritone and tenor.

base.en: Halfway through middle school, Edgar morphed from a soprano to a bass, much to the amazement and amusement of his fellow choristers.
base.definition: One who sings in the bass range.

base.en: The musician swung the bass over his head like an axe and smashed it into the amplifier, creating a discordant howl of noise.
base.definition: An instrument that plays in the bass range, in particular a double bass, bass guitar, electric bass or bass synthesiser.

base.en: The score had been written without the treble and bass, but it was easy to pick out which was which based on the location of the notes on the staff.
base.definition: The clef sign that indicates that the pitch of the notes is below middle C; a bass clef.

base.en: A base estate is one held by services not honourable, or held by villenage. Such a tenure is called base, or low, and the tenant is a base tenant.

base.definition: Of sound, a voice or an instrument, low in pitch or frequency.

basic.en: Rice is a basic for many Asian villagers.
basic.definition: A necessary commodity, a staple requirement.

basic.en: Arithmetic is a basic for the study of mathematics.
basic.definition: An elementary building block, e.g. a fundamental piece of knowledge.

basic.en: The drill sergeants gave him hell in basic.

basic.definition: Necessary, essential for life or some process.

basic.en: The Hotel Sparta’s accommodation is very basic.

basis.en: Cars must be checked on a yearly basis.

basis.definition: A collection of subsets ("basis elements") of a set, such that this collection covers the set, and for any two basis elements which both contain an element of the set, there is a third basis element contained in the intersection of the first two, which also contains that element.

basket.en: A basket of fake fruit adorned the table.
basket.definition: A lightweight container, generally round, open at the top, and tapering toward the bottom.

basket.en: The point guard drove toward the basket.
basket.definition: A circular hoop, from which a net is suspended, which is the goal through which the players try to throw the ball.

basket.en: The last-second basket sealed the victory.
basket.definition: The act of putting the ball through the basket, thereby scoring points.

basket.en: Wait till I catch you, you little basket!

bat.en: You've been in for ages. Can I have a bat now?
bat.definition: A turn at hitting the ball with a bat in a game.

bat.en: He batted the ball away with a satisfying thwack.
bat.definition: To hit with a bat or (figuratively) as if with a bat.

bat.en: The cat batted at the toy.
bat.definition: To strike or swipe as though with a bat.

bat.en: I've spent all week batting around the country.
bat.definition: (usually with 'around' or 'about') To flit quickly from place to place.

bath.en: a bath of heated sand, ashes, steam, or hot air
bath.definition: A substance or preparation in which something is immersed.

bath.definition: A lavatory: a room containing a toilet and (typically but not necessarily) a bathtub.

bathe.en: I'm going to have a midnight bathe tonight.
bathe.definition: The act of swimming or bathing, especially in the sea, a lake, or a river; a swimming bath.

bathe.en: We bathe our baby before going to bed; other parents do it in the morning if they have time.
bathe.definition: To clean a person by immersion in water or using water; to give someone a bath.

bathe.en: She bathed her eyes with liquid to remove the stinging chemical.
bathe.definition: To apply water or other liquid to; to suffuse or cover with liquid.

bathe.en: A dense fog bathed the city streets.
bathe.definition: (transitive and intransitive) To cover or surround.

bathe.en: The women bathed in the sun.

bathroom.en: Most Americans don't know 'WC' and many Brits mock 'bathroom' but almost everyone understands 'toilet' or 'lavatory'.
bathroom.definition: A lavatory: a room containing a toilet and (typically but not necessarily) a bathtub.

battle.definition: To join in battle; to contend in fight

battle.en: She has been battling cancer for years.
battle.definition: To fight or struggle; to enter into a battle with.

bay.definition: To bark at; hence, to follow with barking; to bring or drive to bay.

bean.en: The pitcher beaned the batter, rather than letting him hit another home run.
bean.definition: To hit deliberately with a projectile, especially in the head.

bear.en: That window can be a bear to open.
bear.definition: Something difficult or tiresome; a burden or chore.

bear.en: The great bear market starting in 1929 scared a whole generation of investors.
bear.definition: (investments) Characterized by declining prices in securities markets or by belief that the prices will fall.

bear.en: Judging from the look on his face, he wasn't bearing good news.
bear.definition: To carry or convey, literally or figuratively.

bear.en: By my readings, we're bearing due south, so we should turn about ten degrees east.
bear.definition: (originally nautical) To be, or head, in a specific direction or azimuth (from somewhere).

beard.definition: Long or stiff hairs on a plant; the awn.

beard.en: Robin Hood is always shown as bearding the Sheriff of Nottingham.
beard.definition: To boldly and bravely oppose or confront, often to the chagrin of the one being bearded.

beat.en: a beat of the heart; the beat of the pulse

beat.definition: That which beats, or surpasses, another or others.

beat.en: As soon as she heard that her father had died, she went into a rage and beat the wall with her fists until her knuckles bled.
He danced hypnotically while she beat the atabaque.
beat.definition: To strike or pound repeatedly, usually in some sort of rhythm.

beat.en: I just can't seem to beat the last level of this video game.
beat.definition: To win against; to defeat or overcome; to do better than, outdo, or excel (someone) in a particular, competitive event.

beat.en: Beat the eggs and whip the cream.
beat.definition: To mix food in a rapid fashion. Compare whip.

beat.en: He wanted $50 for it, but I managed to beat him down to $35.
beat.definition: (In haggling for a price) of a buyer, to persuade the seller to reduce a price

beat.en: to beat a retreat; to beat to quarters
beat.definition: To indicate by beating or drumming.
To make a sound when struck.

beat.en: The drummers beat to call soldiers to their quarters.
beat.definition: To make a succession of strokes on a drum.
To arrive at a place before someone.

beat.en: Bruv, she came in just as we started to beat.
He beat me out of 12 bucks last night.

Dude, you drive a beat car like that and you ain’t gonna get no honeys.

beautiful.definition: Someone who is beautiful. Can be used as a term of address.

beautiful.en: Anyone who has ever met her thought she was absolutely beautiful.

beauty.definition: The quality of being (especially visually) attractive, pleasing, fine or good-looking; comeliness.

beauty.definition: Something that is particularly good or pleasing.

beauty.en: The beauty of the deal is it costs nothing!
beauty.definition: (with the definite article) The excellence or genius of a scheme or decision.

beauty.en: He made a beauty pass through the neutral zone.

because.en: My life is ruined because of you!

because.definition: By or for the cause that; on this account that; for the reason that.

because.en: I don't think he is a nice person, because he yells at people for no reason.

become.en: It hath becomen so that many a man had to sterve.
become.definition: To come about; happen; come into being; arise.

become.en: She became a doctor when she was 25.

become.definition: Of an adornment, piece of clothing etc.: to look attractive on (someone).

bed.en:
My cat often sleeps on my bed.
I keep a glass of water next to my bed when I sleep.
bed.definition: A piece of furniture, usually flat and soft, on which to rest or sleep.

bed.en: The meats and cheeses lay on a bed of lettuce.
bed.definition: A place, or flat surface or layer, on which something else rests or is laid.

bedroom.en: Please don't enter my bedroom without knocking.
bedroom.definition: A room in a house where a bed is kept for sleeping.

bee.definition: A contest, especially for spelling; see spelling bee.

bee.en: Cride out, Now now Sir knight, shew what ye bee,

beef.definition: The meat from a cow, bull or other bovine.

beef.en: He's got a beef with everyone in the room.
beef.definition: (plural: beefs) A grudge; dislike (of something or someone); lack of faith or trust (in something or someone); a reason for a dislike or grudge. (often + with)

beef.en: Ugh, who just beefed in here?
Those two are beefing right now - best you stay out of it for now.
beef.definition: To feud or hold a grudge against.

beef.definition: Being a bovine animal that is being raised for its meat.
Producing or known for raising lots of beef.
Consisting of or containing beef as an ingredient.

beer.en: Beer is brewed all over the world.
beer.definition: An alcoholic drink fermented from starch material, commonly barley malt, often with hops or some other substance to impart a bitter flavor.

beer.en: Can I buy you a beer?
beer.definition: A glass, bottle, or can of any of the above beverages.

beer.en: I haven't tried this beer before.

before.en: I want this done before Monday.

before.en: I'll die before I'll tell you anything about it.

beg.en: He begged on the street corner from passers-by.

beg.en: He begged her to go to the prom with him.

beg.en: What was the beginning of the dispute?

beg.en: That house is at the beginning of the street.

begin.en: I began playing the piano at the age of five.   Now that everyone is here, we should begin the presentation.

begin.en: The program begins at 9 o'clock on the dot.    I rushed to get to class on time, but the lesson had already begun.
begin.definition: To be in the first stage of some situation

beginning.en: What was the beginning of the dispute?
beginning.definition: That which begins or originates something; the source or first cause.

beginning.en: That house is at the beginning of the street.
beginning.definition: The initial portion of some extended thing.

beginning.en: I began playing the piano at the age of five.   Now that everyone is here, we should begin the presentation.
beginning.definition: To start, to initiate or take the first step into something.

beginning.en: The program begins at 9 o'clock on the dot.    I rushed to get to class on time, but the lesson had already begun.
beginning.definition: To be in the first stage of some situation

beginning.en: in the beginning paragraph of the chapter
beginning.definition: Of or relating to the first portion of some extended thing.

behave.en: You need to behave yourself, young lady.
behave.definition: To conduct (oneself) well, or in a given way.

belief.en: It's my belief that the thief is somebody known to us.

believe.en: After that night in the church, I believed.

bell.en: I’ll give you a bell later.

bell.definition: To shape so that it flares out like a bell.

belong.definition: (followed by to) To be part of, or the property of.

belong.en: Suppose x belongs to \mathbb{R}...  (— written: x \in \mathbb{R})
belong.definition: (followed by to) To be an element of (a set). The symbol \in means belongs to.

below.en: The treasure is buried two meters below the surface.

belt.en: The motor had a single belt that snaked its way back and forth around a variety of wheels.
belt.definition: A band that is used in a machine to help transfer motion or power.

belt.en: a belt of trees; a belt of sand
belt.definition: Anything that resembles a belt, or that encircles or crosses like a belt; a strip or stripe.

bench.en: They sat on a park bench and tossed bread crumbs to the ducks and pigeons.
bench.definition: A long seat with or without a back, found for example in parks and schools.

bench.en: They are awaiting a decision on the motion from the bench.
bench.definition: The people who decide on the verdict; the judiciary.

bench.en: She sat on the bench for 30 years before she retired.
bench.definition: The place where the judges sit.

bench.en: He spent the first three games on the bench, watching.
bench.definition: The place where players (substitutes) and coaches sit when not playing.

bench.en: I heard he can bench 150 pounds.

bend.en: There's a sharp bend in the road ahead.

bend.definition: (in the plural, underwater diving, with the) A severe condition caused by excessively quick decompression, causing bubbles of nitrogen to form in the blood; decompression sickness.
(in the plural) The frames or ribs that form the ship's body from the keel to the top of the sides.
To cause (something) to change its shape into a curve, by physical force, chemical action, or any other means.

bend.en: Look at the trees bending in the wind.
The road bends to the right
He bent down to pick up the pieces.
They bent me to their will.
I am bending to my desire to eat junk food.

bend.definition: To apply to a task or purpose.

bend.en: He bent to the goal of gaining market share.
bend.definition: To apply oneself to a task or purpose.

bend.definition: To tie, as in securing a line to a cleat; to shackle a chain to an anchor; make fast.

benefit.en: It was for her benefit.   His benefit was free beer.
benefit.definition: An advantage; help or aid from something.

beside.en: A small table beside the bed
beside.definition: Next to; at the side of.

besides.en: I don't feel like going out tonight. Besides, I have to work tomorrow morning anyway.
besides.definition: Used to emphasize an additional point, especially an important or stronger reason; Moreover; furthermore.

besides.en: I have been to Spain but nowhere besides.

besides.definition: Other than; except for; instead of.

best.definition: The supreme effort one can make, or has made.

best.en: I was somewhat distant lately, and my lady promised me head every Tuesday of the week when I'm nice to her, so I better be on my best.
Unpacking took the best part of a week.

The soup is good and hot.
best.definition: (when with and) Very, extremely. See good and.

bet.en: Dylan owes Fletcher $30 from an unsuccessful bet.
bet.definition: A wager, an agreement between two parties that a stake (usually money) will be paid by the loser to the winner (the winner being the one who correctly forecast the outcome of an event).

bet.en: It’s a safe bet that it will rain tomorrow.

Fancy going for a drink after work?
You bet I do!
bet.definition: To be sure of something; to be able to count on something.

better.en: He quickly found Ali his better in the ring.
better.definition: An entity, usually animate, deemed superior to another; one who has a claim to precedence; a superior.

better.en: The soup is good and hot.
better.definition: (when with and) Very, extremely. See good and.

better.definition: (with bare infinitive) Should; ought to; need to; must.

between.en: I want to buy one that costs somewhere between forty and fifty dollars.

between.en: Between you and me, I think the boss is crazy.  Let's keep this between ourselves.

beyond.en: 2017 July 13, Joseph Gamp "Marvel release stirring new set images of Black Panther and we are BEYOND excited" Metro (UK)

beyond.en: The patient was beyond medical help.
beyond.definition: Past, or out of reach of.

beyond.en: He understood geometry well, but algebraic topology was beyond him.

big.en: Elephants are big animals, and they eat a lot.

big.en: That style is very big right now in Europe, especially among teenagers.

big.en: You are a big liar.  Why are you in such a big hurry?
big.definition: Used as an intensifier, especially of negative-valence nouns

big.en: He's always talking big, but he never delivers.

big.definition: In a large amount or to a large extent.

big.en: You've got to think big to succeed at Amalgamated Plumbing.

billion.en: There were billions of people at the concert.

bind.definition: A strong grip or stranglehold on a position that is difficult for the opponent to break.

bind.en: Just to make the cheese more binding
bind.definition: To cohere or stick together in a mass.

bind.en: I wish I knew why the sewing machine binds up after I use it for a while.
bind.definition: To be restrained from motion, or from customary or natural action, as by friction.

bind.en: These are the ties that bind.

bind.en: to bind grain in bundles  to bind a prisoner
bind.definition: To tie or fasten tightly together, with a cord, band, ligature, chain, etc.

bind.en: to bind the conscience  to bind by kindness  bound by affection  commerce binds nations to each other
bind.definition: To oblige, restrain, or hold, by authority, law, duty, promise, vow, affection, or other social tie.

bind.en: to bind an apprentice  bound out to service
bind.definition: To place under legal obligation to serve.

bind.en: to bind a belt about one  to bind a compress upon a wound
bind.definition: To make fast (a thing) about or upon something, as by tying; to encircle with something.

To prevent or restrain from customary or natural action, as by producing constipation.

bind.en: The three novels were bound together.
bind.definition: To put together in a cover, as of books.

bird.definition: A member of the class of animals Aves in the phylum Chordata, characterized by being warm-blooded, having feathers and wings usually capable of flight, and laying eggs.

bird.en: Mike went out with his bird last night.

birth.en: Intersex babies account for roughly one per cent of all births.

birth.definition: A beginning or start; a point of origin.

birth.en: He was of noble birth, but fortune had not favored him.
birth.definition: The circumstances of one's background, ancestry, or upbringing.

birth.en: Her birth father left when she was a baby; she was raised by her mother and stepfather.
birth.definition: A familial relationship established by childbirth.

bit.en: A horse hates having a bit put in its mouth.
bit.definition: A piece of metal placed in a horse's mouth and connected to the reins to direct the animal.

bit.en: a threepenny bit
bit.definition: A coin of a specified value.

bit.en: A quarter is two bits.
bit.definition: A unit of currency or coin in the Americas worth a fraction of a Spanish dollar; now specifically, an eighth of a US dollar.

bit.en: There were bits of paper all over the floor.   Does your leg still hurt? / Just a bit now.   I've done my bit; I expect you to do yours.
bit.definition: A small amount of something.

bit.en: The 400 metres race was won in 47 seconds and bits.
bit.definition: (plural) Fractions of a second.

bit.en: I'd like a big bit of cake, please.
bit.definition: A portion of something.

bit.en: Am I bored? Not a bit of it!

bit.definition: An excerpt of material making up part of a show, comedy routine, etc.

bit.en: Even though he's bit, of course the zombies would still chase him.
bit.definition: Bitten.

bit.en: That dog is about to bite!
bit.definition: To attack with the teeth.

bit.en: If you see me, come and say hello. I don't bite.

bit.definition: To take hold; to establish firm contact with.

bit.definition: (of a fish) To bite a baited hook or other lure and thus be caught.

cat.en: Just as a monoid consists of an underlying set with a binary operation "on top of it" which is closed, associative and with an identity, a category consists of an underlying digraph with an arrow composition operation "on top of it" which is transitively closed, associative, and with an identity at each object. In fact, a category's composition operation, when restricted to a single one of its objects, turns that object's set of arrows (which would all be loops) into a monoid.
cat.definition: A collection of objects, together with a transitively closed collection of composable arrows between them, such that every object has an identity arrow, and such that arrow composition is associative.

The fourth apocalyptic rider (Bible, revelations 6:8) is male θᾰ́νᾰτος (thanatos) in Greek. It has the female name Mors in Latin, but is referred to with male forms qui and eum. The following quotes show this rider on a pale horse is his in the English Bible and she in Peter Gabriel's lyrics.

heavy.en: With his wrinkled, uneven face, the actor always seemed to play the heavy in films.
heavy.definition: A villain or bad guy; the one responsible for evil or aggressive acts.

heavy.en: A fight started outside the bar but the heavies came out and stopped it.
The union was well known for the methods it used to heavy many businesses.
heavy.definition: To use power and/or wealth to exert influence on, e.g., governments or corporations; to pressure.

heavy.en: heavy yokes, expenses, undertakings, trials, news, etc.
heavy.definition: Not easy to bear; burdensome; oppressive.

heavy.en: The Moody Blues are, like, heavy.
Come heavy, or not at all.
He was a heavy sleeper, a heavy eater and a heavy smoker – certainly not an ideal husband.
heavy.definition: (of a person) Doing the specified activity more intensely than most other people.

heavy.en: Cheese-stuffed sausage is too heavy to eat before exercising.
heavy.definition: (of food) High in fat or protein; difficult to digest.

heavy.en: it was a heavy storm;  a heavy slumber in bed;  a heavy punch
heavy.definition: Of great force, power, or intensity; deep or intense.

heavy.en: his eyes were heavy with sleep;  she was heavy with child
a heavy gait, looks, manners, style, etc.
heavy.definition: Slow; sluggish; inactive; or lifeless, dull, inanimate, stupid.

heavy.en: a heavy road; a heavy soil

heavy.definition: In a heavy manner; weightily; heavily; gravely.

height.en: She's at the height of her career.
height.definition: The highest point or maximum degree.

helicopter.en: We flew over the city in a helicopter.
helicopter.definition: An aircraft that is borne along by one or more sets of long rotating blades which allow it to hover, move in any direction including reverse, or land; and typically having a smaller set of blades on its tail that stabilize the aircraft.

helicopter.en: helicoptering his jacket, helicoptering his arms
helicopter.definition: To rotate like a helicopter blade.

hello.definition: A greeting (salutation) said when meeting someone or acknowledging someone’s arrival or presence.

hello.en: Hello? How may I help you?
hello.definition: A greeting used when answering the telephone.
A call for response if it is not clear if anyone is present or listening, or if a telephone conversation may have been disconnected.

hello.en: You just tried to start your car with your cell phone. Hello?
hello.definition: Used sarcastically to imply that the person addressed or referred to has done something the speaker or writer considers to be foolish.
An expression of puzzlement or discovery.

help.en: I need some help with my homework.
help.definition: Action given to provide assistance; aid.

help.en: He was a great help to me when I was moving house.
help.definition: (usually uncountable) Something or someone which provides assistance with a task.

help.en: I can't find anything in the help about rotating an image.
help.definition: Documentation provided with computer software, etc. and accessed using the computer.

help.en: Most of the hired help is seasonal, for the harvest.
help.definition: (usually uncountable) One or more people employed to help in the maintenance of a house or the operation of a farm or enterprise.

help.en: He really needs help in handling customer complaints.
help.definition: Correction of deficits, as by psychological counseling or medication or social support or remedial training.

help.en: He helped his grandfather cook breakfast.
help.definition: To provide assistance to (someone or something).

help.en: Help yourself to whatever's in the fridge.
help.definition: To assist (a person) in getting something, especially food or drink at table; used with to.

help.en: If you want to get a job, it helps to have some prior experience.
help.definition: To contribute in some way to.

help.en: Are they going to beat us? Not if I can help it!
help.definition: To avoid; to prevent; to refrain from; to restrain (oneself). Usually used in nonassertive contexts with can.

help.en:
— Take that, you scoundrel.
— Help! Robin, help!
help.definition: A cry of distress or an urgent request for assistance

her.en: I think this bird is a him, but it may be a her.

here.en: An Alzheimer patient's here may in his mind be anywhere he called home in the time he presently re-lives.

here.definition: Filler after a noun or demonstrative pronoun, solely for emphasis.

here.en: This here orange is too sour.
here.definition: Filler after a demonstrative pronoun but before the noun it modifies, solely for emphasis.
(location) In, on, or at this place.
(location) To this place; used in place of the more dated hither.

here.en: Derivatives can refer to anything that is derived from something else, but here they refer specifically to functions that give the slope of the tangent line to a curve.

here.definition: At this point in the argument, narration, or other, usually written, work.

here.en: Here, now I'm giving it to you.
here.definition: Used semi-assertively to offer something to the listener.

here.en: Here, I'm tired and I want a drink.
here.definition: Used for emphasis at the beginning of a sentence when expressing an opinion or want.

hero.en: Let's discuss how to play if the hero has KK, and there's an ace on board.
hero.definition: The current player, especially an hypothetical player for example and didactic purposes. Compare: villain. Not to be confused with hero call.

hers.en: I think this bird is a him, but it may be a her.

herself.definition: (reflexive pronoun) Her; the female object of a verb or preposition that also appears as the subject.
She; an intensive repetition of the female subject, often used to indicate the exclusiveness of that person as the only satisfier of the predicate.

herself.en: Have you seen herself yet this morning?
herself.definition: The subject or non-reflexive object of a predicate; she (used of upper-class ladies, or sarcastically, of women who imagine themselves to be more important than others)

hesitate.en: He hesitated whether to accept the offer or not; men often hesitate in forming a judgment.
hesitate.definition: To stop or pause respecting decision or action; to be in suspense or uncertainty as to a determination.

hide.en: He hides his magazines under the bed.
hide.definition: To put (something) in a place where it will be harder to discover or out of sight.

high.en: It was one of the highs of his career.
high.definition: A point of success or achievement; a time when things are at their best.

high.en: That pill gave me a high for a few hours, before I had a comedown.
high.definition: A period of euphoria, from excitement or from an intake of drugs.

high.en: A large high is centred on the Azores.
high.definition: A large area of elevated atmospheric pressure; an anticyclone.

high.en: Inflation reached a ten-year high.
high.definition: The maximum value attained by some quantity within a specified period.

high.en: Today's high was 32°C.
high.definition: The maximum atmospheric temperature recorded at a particular location, especially during one 24-hour period.

high.en: The sun higheth.
high.definition: To rise.

high.en: The balloon rose high in the sky.   The wall was high.   a high mountain
high.definition: Very elevated; extending or being far above a base; tall; lofty.

high.en: three feet high   three Mount Everests high
high.definition: Having a specified elevation or height; tall.

high.en: The oldest of the elves' royal family still conversed in High Elvish.
high.definition: Elevated in status, esteem, prestige; exalted in rank, station, or character.

high.en: high crimes, the high festival of the sun
high.definition: Of great importance and consequence: grave (if negative) or solemn (if positive).

high.en: high (i.e. intense) heat; high (i.e. full or quite) noon; high (i.e. rich or spicy) seasoning; high (i.e. complete) pleasure; high (i.e. deep or vivid) colour; high (i.e. extensive, thorough) scholarship; high tide; high [tourism] season; the High Middle Ages
high.definition: Consummate; advanced (e.g. in development) to the utmost extent or culmination, or possessing a quality in its supreme degree, at its zenith.

high.en: high latitude, high antiquity
high.definition: (in several set phrases) Remote in distance or time.

high.en: in high spirits
high.definition: Elevated in mood; marked by great merriment, excitement, etc.

high.en: high living, the high life
high.definition: (of a lifestyle) Luxurious; rich.

high.en: a high tone
high.definition: Lofty, often to the point of arrogant, haughty, boastful, proud.

high.en: My bank charges me a high interest rate.   I was running a high temperature and had high cholesterol.   high voltage   high prices   high winds   a high number
high.definition: Large, great (in amount or quantity, value, force, energy, etc).

high.en: The note was too high for her to sing.
high.definition: (acoustics) Acute or shrill in pitch, due to being of greater frequency, i.e. produced by more rapid vibrations (wave oscillations).

high.en: Epicures do not cook game before it is high.
high.definition: (of meat, especially venison) Strong-scented; slightly tainted/spoiled; beginning to decompose.

high.en: How high above land did you fly?
high.definition: In or to an elevated position.

high.en: Costs have grown higher this year again.
high.definition: In or at a great value.

high.en: I certainly can't sing that high.
high.definition: At a pitch of great frequency.

highlight.en: Meeting my future wife was the highlight of my trip to Spain.
highlight.definition: An especially significant or interesting detail or event or period of time.

highly.definition: In a high or esteemed manner.

highly.en: He is in a highly visible job.

hill.en: The park is sheltered from the wind by a hill to the east.
hill.definition: An elevated location smaller than a mountain.

hill.en: You need to pick up speed to get up the hill that's coming up.
a hill of corn or potatoes
hill.definition: A single cluster or group of plants growing close together, and having the earth heaped up about them.

him.en: I think this bird is a him, but it may be a her.

himself.definition: (reflexive pronoun) Him; the male object of a verb or preposition that also appears as the subject
He; used as an intensifier, often to emphasize that the referent is the exclusive participant in the predicate

himself.en: Has himself come down to breakfast yet?
himself.definition: The subject or non-reflexive object of a predicate; he (used of upper-class gentlemen, or sarcastically, of men who imagine themselves to be more important than others)

hire.en: The sign offered pedalos on hire.
hire.definition: Payment for the temporary use of something.

hire.en: When my grandfather retired, he had over twenty mechanics in his hire.
hire.definition: The state of being hired, or having a job; employment.

hire.en: We pair up each of our new hires with one of our original hires.
hire.definition: A person who has been hired, especially in a cohort.

hire.en: We hired a car for two weeks because ours had broken down.
hire.definition: To obtain the services of in return for fixed payment.

hire.en: The company had problems when it tried to hire more skilled workers.
hire.definition: To employ; to obtain the services of (a person) in exchange for remuneration; to give someone a job.

hire.en: They hired themselves out as day laborers.  They hired out their basement for Inauguration week.
hire.definition: To exchange the services of for remuneration.

hire.en: After waiting two years for her husband to finish the tiling, she decided to hire it done.
hire.definition: To accomplish by paying for services.

hire.en: They hired out as day laborers.

his.en: The decision was his to live with.
his.definition: That which belongs to him; the possessive case of he, used without a following noun.

his.en: I didn't even get a hi.
his.definition: The word "hi" used as a greeting.

historic.definition: Very important; noteworthy: having importance or significance in history.

historic.en: The historic tenses include the imperfect, the pluperfect, and the future perfect.
historic.definition: (grammar) Various grammatical tenses and moods specially used in retelling past events.

historic.en: July 4, 1776, is a historic date. A great deal of historical research has been done on the events leading up to that day.
historic.definition: Of, concerning, or in accordance with recorded history, (particularly) as opposed to legends, myths, and fictions.
Of, concerning, or in accordance with the scholarly discipline of history.
Forming compound adjectives with the meaning "historical/~" or "historically":

historical.en: July 4, 1776, is a historic date. A great deal of historical research has been done on the events leading up to that day.
historical.definition: Of, concerning, or in accordance with recorded history, (particularly) as opposed to legends, myths, and fictions.
Of, concerning, or in accordance with the scholarly discipline of history.
Forming compound adjectives with the meaning "historical/~" or "historically":

history.en: History repeats itself if we don’t learn from its mistakes.
He teaches history at the university.   History will not look kindly on these tyrants.   He dreams of an invention that will make history.
history.definition: The branch of knowledge that studies the past; the assessment of notable events.

history.en: What is your medical history?   The family's history includes events best forgotten.
history.definition: A set of events involving an entity.

history.en: I really enjoyed Shakespeare's tragedies more than his histories.
history.definition: A record or narrative description of past events.

history.en: A personal medical history is required for the insurance policy.   He has a history of cancer in his family.
history.definition: A list of past and continuing medical conditions of an individual or family.

history.en: I visited a great site yesterday but forgot the URL. Luckily, I didn't clear my history.
history.definition: A record of previous user events, especially of visited web pages in a browser.

history.en: I told him that if he doesn't get his act together, he's history.
history.definition: Something that no longer exists or is no longer relevant.

history.en: He has had a lot of history with the police.

hit.definition: A blow; a punch; a striking against; the collision of one body against another; the stroke that touches anything.

hit.definition: The complete play, when the batter reaches base without the benefit of a walk, error, or fielder’s choice.

To manage to touch (a target) in the right place.

hit.en: Somebody's been here! Hit the lights!
We hit the grocery store on the way to the park.
You'll hit some nasty thunderstorms if you descend too late.  We hit a lot of traffic coming back from the movies.

hit.en: The economy was hit by a recession.  The hurricane hit his fishing business hard.

hold.en: Keep a firm hold on the handlebars.

hold.definition: The percentage the house wins on a gamble, the house or bookmaker's hold.

hold.en: As of Monday night the total Melbourne Cup hold was $848,015
hold.definition: The wager amount, the total hold.

hold.en: Elections will be held on the first Sunday of next month.
hold.definition: To organise an event or meeting (usually in passive voice).

hold.en: A martini, please, and hold the olive.

The cargo area of a ship or aircraft (often holds or cargo hold).

hole.en: There’s a hole in my shoe.  Her stocking has a hole in it.

An orifice, in particular the anus. When used with shut it always refers to the mouth.

hole.en: Are you going out to get your hole tonight?
hole.definition: (particularly in the phrase "get one's hole") Sex, or a sex partner.
An undesirable place to live or visit; a hovel.

hole.en: If you find yourself in a hole, stop digging.

hole.definition: To make holes in (an object or surface).

hole.en: Woods holed a standard three foot putt
hole.definition: To drive into a hole, as an animal, or a billiard ball or golf ball.

hole.en: to hole a post for the insertion of rails or bars

hole.en: He is of whole mind, but the same cannot be said about his physical state.

hole.definition: (of food) From which none of its constituents has been removed.

holiday.definition: A day on which a festival, religious event, or national celebration is traditionally observed.

holiday.en: I want to take a French course this summer holiday.
holiday.definition: A period during which pupils do not attend their school; often plural; rarely used for students at university (usually: vacation).
A period during which, by agreement, the usual payments are not made.

holy.en: I'm planning to visit the holy city of Jerusalem this Christmas.
holy.definition: Dedicated to a religious purpose or a god.

holy.en: My grandmother is a very holy woman.
holy.definition: Morally perfect or flawless, or nearly so.

holy.en: Holy cow, I can’t believe he actually lost the race!
holy.definition: Used as an intensifier in various interjections.

home.definition: The locality where a thing is usually found, or was first found, or where it is naturally abundant; habitat; seat.
(of animals) To return to its owner.

home.en: The missile was able to home in on the target.
home.definition: (always with "in on") To seek or aim for something.

home.en: the home end, home advantage, home supporters
home.definition: Relating to the home team (the team at whose venue a game is played).

home.en: Everyone's gone to watch the game; there's nobody home.
home.definition: At or in one's place of residence or one's customary or official location; at home
Into the right, proper or stowed position

honest.en: We’re the most honest people you will ever come across.
honest.definition: (of a person or institution) Scrupulous with regard to telling the truth; not given to swindling, lying, or fraud; upright.
(of a statement) True, especially as far as is known by the person making the statement; fair; unbiased.
Earned or acquired in a fair manner.

honor.en: The crowds gave the returning general much honor and praise.
honor.definition: Recognition of importance or value; respect; veneration (of someone, usually for being morally upright or successful)

honor.en: He was a most perfect knight, for he had great honor and chivalry.
honor.definition: The state of being morally upright, honest, noble, virtuous, and magnanimous; excellence of character; the perception of such a state; favourable reputation; dignity

honor.en: Audie Murphy received many honors, such as the Distinguished Service Cross.
honor.definition: A token of praise or respect; something that represents praiseworthiness or respect, such as a prize or award given by the state to a citizen

honor.en: I had the honour of dining with the ambassador.
I'll let you have the honours, Bob—go ahead.
honor.definition: (in the plural) the privilege of going first

honor.en: He is an honour to his nation.
honor.definition: A cause of respect and fame; a glory; an excellency; an ornament.

honor.en: At university I took honours in modern history.
honor.definition: (in the plural) (courses for) an honours degree: a university qualification of the highest rank

honor.en: The freedom fighters will be forever remembered and honored by the people.

honor.en: I trusted you, but you have not honored your promise.

honor.definition: To make payment in respect of (a cheque, banker's draft, etc.)

hook.en: He is not handling this job, so we're giving him the hook.

hook.en: We've added hooks to allow undefined message types to be handled with custom code.

hook.en: Hook the bag here, and the conveyor will carry it away.

hook.definition: To catch with a hook (hook a fish).

hook.en: He hooked his fingers through his belt loops.
hook.definition: To insert in a curved way reminiscent of a hook.

hook.en: A free trial is a good way to hook customers.
hook.definition: To ensnare or obligate someone, as if with a hook.

hook.en: If you hook your network cable into the jack, you'll be on the network.

hook.definition: (usually in passive) To make addicted; to captivate.

hook.en: The opposing team's forward hooked me, but the referee didn't see it, so no penalty.
hook.definition: To engage in the illegal maneuver of hooking (i.e., using the hockey stick to trip or block another player)

hook.en: I had a cheap flat in the bad part of town, and I could watch the working girls hooking from my bedroom window.

hope.en: I am still hoping that all will turn out well.
hope.definition: To want something to happen, with a sense of expectation that it might.

hope.en: I hope you all the best.

hope.en: We still have one hope left: my roommate might see the note I left on the table.
hope.definition: A person or thing that is a source of hope.

horizon.definition: The visible horizontal line or point (in all directions) that appears to connect the Earth to the sky.

horizon.definition: The range or limit of one's knowledge, experience or interest; a boundary or threshold.

horror.en: The neighbour's kids are a pack of little horrors!

host.en: A good host is always considerate of the guest’s needs.

host.definition: (evolution) An organism bearing certain genetic material.

hot.en: He forgot that the frying pan was hot and burned his hand.

hot.en: It is hotter in summer than in winter.

hot.en: Aren't you hot with that thick coat on?

hot.definition: (of a temper) Easily provoked to anger.

hot.definition: (of a person) Very physically and/or sexually attractive.

hot.definition: Of great current interest; provoking current debate or controversy.

hot.en: Am I warm yet? — You're hot!

hot.en: He was finished in a hot minute.

hot.en: I wouldn't speed through here if I was you. This area is hot this time of night.
hot.definition: Characterized by police presence or activity.

hot.en: I wouldn't trust him. He gave me a hot check last week.
hot.definition: (of a draft/check) Not covered by funds on account.

hour.en: I spent an hour at lunch.
hour.definition: A time period of sixty minutes; one twenty-fourth of a day.

hour.en: The hour grows late and I must go home.

hour.definition: A distance that can be traveled in one hour.

house.en: This is my house and my family's ancestral home.
house.definition: A structure built or serving as an abode of human beings.

house.en: On arriving at the zoo, we immediately headed for the monkey house.
house.definition: A building used for something other than a residence (typically with qualifying word).

house.en: After her swan-song, there wasn't a dry eye in the house.

house.en: A curse lay upon the House of Atreus.

house.en: I was a member of Spenser house when I was at school.

house.en: As the babysitter, Emma always acted as the mother whenever the kids demanded to play house.

house.en: The car is housed in the garage.

To stow in a safe place; to take down and make safe.

household.en: a household word; a household name
household.definition: Widely known to the public; familiar.

housing.en: The car is housed in the garage.
housing.definition: To keep within a structure or container.
To stow in a safe place; to take down and make safe.

housing.en: The gears were grinding against their housing.
housing.definition: A mechanical component's container or covering.

how.en: I am not interested in the why, but in the how.

how.en: How do you solve this puzzle?

how.en: I remember how I solved this puzzle.

however.en: He told me not to do it. I, however, did it anyway. / I did it anyway, however. / (sometimes proscribed:) However, I did it anyway.

however.en: However clear you think you've been, many questions will remain.

she offered to help however she could

huge.en: Our next album is going to be huge!  In our league our coach is huge!
huge.definition: Distinctly interesting, significant, important, likeable, well regarded.

human.en: Humans share common ancestors with other apes.
human.definition: A human being, whether man, woman or child.

human.en: To err is human; to forgive, divine.
human.definition: Having the nature or attributes of a human being.

humor.en: She has a great sense of humour, and I always laugh a lot whenever we get together.
humor.definition: The quality of being amusing, comical, funny.

hundred.en: He made a hundred in the historic match.

hunger.en: I have a hunger to win.

light.definition: A traffic light, or, by extension, an intersection controlled by one or more that will face a traveler who is receiving instructions.

light.en: We lit the fire to get some heat.
light.definition: To start (a fire).

light.definition: To set fire to; to set burning.

light.definition: To illuminate; to provide light for when it is dark.

light.en: This soggy match will not light.
light.definition: To become ignited; to take fire.

light.definition: To make (a bonus) available to be collected by hitting a target, and thus light up the feature light corresponding to that bonus to indicate its availability.

light.en: The room is light when the Sun shines through the window.
light.definition: Having light; bright; clear; not dark or obscure.

light.en: She had light skin.
light.definition: Pale or whitish in color; highly luminous and more or less deficient in chroma.

light.en: I like my coffee light.
light.definition: (of coffee) Served with extra milk or cream.

light.en: a light load ; a lighter backpack after having removed the books ; light weapons
light.definition: Having little or relatively little actual weight; not cumbrous or unwieldy.

light.en: feathers and cork are light ; oil is lighter than water
light.definition: Having little weight as compared with bulk; of little density or specific gravity.

light.en: to issue light coin
light.definition: Of short or insufficient weight; weighing less than the legal, standard or proper amount; clipped or diminished.

light.en: a light bread ; sponge cake is a light cake
light.definition: Not heavy or soggy; spongy; well raised.

light.en: This artist clearly had a light, flowing touch.
light.definition: Gentle; having little force or momentum.

light.en: light duties around the house
light.definition: Easy to endure or perform.

light.en: This light beer still gets you drunk if you have enough of it.
light.definition: Low in fat, calories, alcohol, salt, etc.

light.en: I made some light comment, and we moved on.
light.definition: Unimportant, trivial, having little value or significance.

light.en: a light, vain person; a light mind
light.definition: Easily influenced by trifling considerations; unsteady; unsettled; volatile.

light.en: Ogden Nash was a writer of light verse.
light.definition: Indulging in, or inclined to, levity; lacking dignity or solemnity; frivolous; airy.

light.en: light sleep; light anesthesia
light.definition: Easily interrupted by stimulation.

light.en: I prefer to travel light.
light.definition: Carrying little.

light.en: I lit upon a rare book in a second-hand bookseller's.
light.definition: To find by chance.

light.en: She fell out of the window but luckily lit on her feet.
light.definition: To alight; to land or come down.

like.en: Tell me your likes and dislikes.
like.definition: (usually in the plural) Something that a person likes (prefers).
To enjoy, be pleased by; favor; be in favor of.

like.en: I like to go to the dentist every six months.
like.definition: To prefer and maintain (an action) as a regular habit or activity.

like.en: He liked to have been too late.
like.definition: To come near; to avoid with difficulty; to escape narrowly.

like.en: I really like Sandra but don't know how to tell her.
like.definition: To find attractive; to prefer the company of; to have mild romantic feelings for.

like.en: I can't stand Bloggs' tomato ketchup, but I liked it on Facebook so I could enter a competition.
like.definition: To show support for, or approval of, something posted on the Internet by marking it with a vote.

like.en: We could go to the museum if you like.
like.definition: (with 'would' and in certain other phrases) To want, desire. See also would like.

like.en: It was something the likes of which I had never seen before.
like.definition: (sometimes as the likes of') Someone similar to a given person, or something similar to a given object; a comparative; a type; a sort.

like.en: My partner and I have like minds.

like.en: It looks like a hot summer in Europe.

likely.en: Rain is likely later this afternoon.
likely.definition: Probable; having a greater-than-even chance of occurring

likely.en: He is likely to succeed at anything he tries.
likely.definition: (as predicate, followed by to and infinitive) Reasonably to be expected; apparently destined, probable

likely.en: Jones is a likely candidate for management.

Plausible; within the realm of credibility

limit.en: There are several existing limits to executive power.
limit.definition: A restriction; a bound beyond which one may not go.

limit.en: The sequence of reciprocals has zero as its limit.
limit.definition: A value to which a sequence converges. Equivalently, the common value of the upper limit and the lower limit of a sequence: if the upper and lower limits are different, then the sequence has no limit (i.e., does not converge).

limit.en: Category theory defines a very general concept of limit.
limit.definition: Any of several abstractions of this concept of limit.

limit.en: the limit of a walk, of a town, or of a country
limit.definition: The final, utmost, or furthest point; the border or edge.

limit.en: The sequence limits on the point a.

limitation.en: Getting into his wheelchair after his amputation, it felt like a limitation you could roll in.
limitation.definition: A restriction; a boundary, real or metaphorical, caused by some thing or some circumstance.

limitation.en: The lawyer obtained impunity by dragging his obviously guilty client's case beyond the ten-year limitation.
limitation.definition: A time period after which some legal action may no longer be brought.

line.en: The arrow descended in a curved line.
line.definition: A path through two or more points (compare ‘segment’); a continuous mark, including as made by a pen; any path, curved or straight.
A hose or pipe, of any size.

line.en: I tried to make a call, but the line was dead.
line.definition: The wire connecting one telegraphic station with another, a telephone or internet cable between two points: a telephone or network connection.
A letter, a written form of communication.
A connected series of public conveyances, as a roadbed or railway track; and hence, an established arrangement for forwarding merchandise, etc.

line.en: The line forms on the right.
line.definition: A more-or-less straight sequence of people, objects, etc., either arranged as a queue or column and often waiting to be processed or dealt with, or arranged abreast of one another in a row (and contrasted with a column), as in a military formation.

line.en: Remember, your answers must match the party line.
line.definition: The official, stated position (or set of positions) of an individual or group, particularly a political or religious faction.

line.en: How many buses does the line have?
line.definition: A set of products or services sold by a business, or by extension, the business itself.

line.en: the engine is in line / out of line
line.definition: Proper relative position or adjustment (of parts, not as to design or proportion, but with reference to smooth working).
To place (objects) into a line (usually used with "up"); to form into a line; to align.
To place persons or things along the side of for security or defense; to strengthen by adding; to fortify.
To mark with a line or lines, to cover with lines.
To read or repeat line by line.

line.en: Jones lined to left in his last at-bat.
line.definition: To hit a line drive; to hit a line drive which is caught for an out. Compare fly and ground.

line.en: The bird lines its nest with soft grass.
line.definition: To cover the inner surface of (something), originally especially with linen.

link.en: Click the hyperlink to go to the next page.
link.definition: Some text or a graphic in an electronic document that can be activated to display another document or trigger an action.

link.en: Copy the hyperlink and paste it into an email.
link.definition: (by extension) An address, URL, or program that defines a hyperlink's function.

link.en: The mayor’s assistant serves as the link to the media.
link.definition: A connection between places, people, events, things, or ideas.

link.en: The third link of the silver chain needs to be resoldered.
link.definition: One element of a chain or other connected series.

link.en: A by-N-link is composed of N lanes.
link.definition: The connection between buses or systems.
Anything doubled and closed like a link of a chain.

link.en: My homepage links to my wife's.
link.definition: (of a Web page) To contain a hyperlink to another page.

link.en: Haven't you seen his Web site? I'll link you to it.
link.definition: To supply (somebody) with a hyperlink; to direct by means of a link.

link.en: Stop linking those unfunny comics all the time!

lion.en: Tigers and lions share a common ancestor from a few million years ago.
lion.definition: A big cat, Panthera leo, native to Africa, India and formerly much of Europe.

list.definition: To cover with list, or with strips of cloth; to put list on; to stripe as if with list.
To cut away a narrow strip, as of sapwood, from the edge of.

list.en: the steady wind listed the ship
list.definition: To cause (something) to tilt to one side.

listen.en: Give the motor a listen and tell me if it sounds off.

listen.definition: To pay attention to a sound or speech.

listen.en: You should listen for the starting gun.
listen.definition: To expect or wait for a sound, such as a signal.

listen.en: Listen, the only reason I yelled at you was because I was upset, OK?  Good children listen to their parents.
listen.definition: To accept advice or obey instruction; to agree or assent.

literary.definition: Relating to writers, or the profession of literature.

literature.definition: Written fiction of a high standard.

little.en: Can I try a little of that sauce?

little.definition: Small in amount or number, having few members.

little.en: I feel better after my little sleep.

live.en: He's not expected to live for more than a few months.
live.definition: To be alive; to have life.

live.en: I live at 2a Acacia Avenue.  He lives in LA, but he's staying here over the summer.

live.en: Her memory lives in that song.

live.en: You'll just have to live with it!  I can't live in a world without you.

live.en: It is difficult to live in poverty.   And they lived happily ever after.

live.en: To live an idle or a useful life.
live.definition: To spend, as one's life; to pass; to maintain; to continue in, constantly or habitually.

live.definition: To outlast danger; to float (said of a ship, boat, etc).

live.en: It is hard to live on the minimum wage.   They lived on stale bread.

live.en: The post office will not ship live animals.

live.en: He is a live example of the consequences of excessive drinking.

live.en: live feathers

live.en: a live ball

loan.en: He made a payment on his loan.

local.definition: Of or pertaining to a restricted part of an organism.

lock.en: If you put the brakes on too hard, the wheels will lock.

lose.en: I lost my way in the forest.

lot.definition: (definite, the lot) All members of a set; everything.

of.en: I have a house and a car.

of.definition: (transitive with adjective or adjective-phrase complement) To depict as being.

of.definition: (obsolete outside Ireland) To be able to speak (a language).

of.definition: (often with present participle) To allow; to tolerate.

of.en: I have two contacts on my scope.
of.definition: To get a reading, measurement, or result from an instrument or calculation.

of.en: We'll schedule closing arguments for Thursday, and the jury will have the case by that afternoon.
of.definition: (of a jury) To consider a court proceeding that has been completed; to begin deliberations on a case.

off.en: He has been very obviously an untrustworthy narrator right from the off.
off.definition: (usually in phrases such as 'from the off', 'at the off', etc.) Beginning; starting point.

off.en: He got in the way so I had him offed.
off.definition: To kill.

off.en: Can you off the light?
off.definition: To switch off.

off.en: All the lights are off.
off.definition: Inoperative, disabled.

off.en: The party's off because the hostess is sick.
off.definition: Cancelled; not happening.

off.en: The drink spilled out of the bottle because the top was off.
off.definition: Not fitted; not being worn.

off.en: This milk is off!
off.definition: Rancid, rotten, gone bad.

off.en: sales are off this quarter
off.definition: Less than normal, in temperament or in result.

off.en: I felt that his comments were a bit off.
off.definition: Inappropriate; untoward.

off.en: He took me down the corridor and into an off room.
off.definition: Far; off to the side.

off.en: He took an off day for fishing.  an off year in politics; the off season
off.definition: Designating a time when one is not strictly attentive to business or affairs, or is absent from a post, and, hence, a time when affairs are not urgent.

off.en: — I'll have the chicken please.
off.definition: (of a dish on a menu) Presently unavailable.

off.en: The off front wheel came loose.
off.definition: (in relation to a vehicle) On the side furthest from the kerb (the right-hand side if one drives on the left).

off.definition: In a direction away from the speaker or object.

off.en: Please switch off the light when you leave.

off.en: He bit off the end of the carrot.
off.definition: So as to remove or separate, or be removed or separated.

off.en: He's off the roof now.
off.definition: Not positioned upon; away from a position upon.

off.en: He was thrown off the team for cheating.
off.definition: Detached, separated, excluded or disconnected from; away from a position of attachment or connection to.

off.en: His office is off this corridor on the right.
off.definition: Used to indicate the location or direction of one thing relative to another, implying adjacency or accessibility via.

off.en: The island is 23 miles off the cape.
off.definition: Used to express location at sea relative to land or mainland.

off.en: There's 20% off the list price.
off.definition: Removed or subtracted from.

off.en: He's been off his feed since Tuesday.
off.definition: No longer wanting or taking.

off.en: He didn't buy it off him. He stole it off him.
off.definition: (more properly 'from') Out of the possession of.

off.en: I'd like to re-order those printer cartridges, let's say 5-off.
off.definition: Placed after a number (of products or parts, as if a unit), in commerce or engineering.

offend.definition: To hurt the feelings of; to displease; to make angry; to insult.

offend.en: Don't worry. I don't offend easily.
offend.definition: To feel or become offended; to take insult.

offend.en: Physically enjoyable frivolity can still offend the conscience
offend.definition: To annoy, cause discomfort or resent.

offensive.en: The Marines today launched a major offensive.

offensive.definition: The posture of attacking or being able to attack.
Causing offense; arousing a visceral reaction of disgust, anger, or hatred.

offensive.en: The army's offensive capabilities. An offensive weapon.
offensive.definition: Relating to an offense or attack, as opposed to defensive.

offensive.en: The offensive coordinator is responsible for ordering all rushing plays.
offensive.definition: Having to do with play directed at scoring.

offer.en: I decline your offer to contract.
offer.definition: A proposal that has been made.
Something put forth, bid, proffered or tendered.

offer.en: His first letter was not a real offer, but an attempt to determine interest.
offer.definition: An invitation to enter into a binding contract communicated to another party which contains terms sufficiently definite to create an enforceable contract if the other party accepts the invitation.

offer.en: She offered to help with her homework.
offer.definition: To propose or express one's willingness (to do something).
To present in words; to proffer; to make a proposal of; to suggest.

offer.en: He offered use of his car for the week.  He offered his good will for the Councilman's vote.
offer.definition: To place at someone’s disposal; to present (something) to be either accepted or turned down.

offer.en: I offered twenty dollars for it. The company is offering a salary of £30,000 a year.
offer.definition: To bid, as a price, reward, or wages.
To put in opposition to; to manifest in an offensive way; to threaten.

office.en: When the office of Secretary of State is vacant, its duties fall upon an official within the department.
She held office as secretary of state until she left office to run for office.
office.definition: Official position, particularly high employment within government; tenure in such a position.

office.en: The office of the Secretary of State is cleaned when it is vacant.
office.definition: A room, set of rooms, or building used for non-manual work, particularly:

office.en: The whole office was there... well, except you, of course.
He's from our public relations office.
office.definition: (in large organizations) The administrative departments housed in such places, particularly:

official.en: David Barnes was the official charged with the running of the sports club.
official.definition: An office holder invested with powers and authorities.

official.en: In most soccer games there are three officials: the referee and two linesmen.
official.definition: A person responsible for applying the rules of a game or sport in a competition.
Of or pertaining to an office or public trust.
Derived from the proper office or officer, or from the proper authority; made or communicated by virtue of authority
(pharmaceutical) Sanctioned by the pharmacopoeia; appointed to be used in medicine; officinal.

official.en: Well, it's official: you lost your mind!

often.en: I often walk to work when the weather is nice.

oil.en: I prefer to paint in oil

old.en: A civilised society should always look after the old in the community.
old.definition: (with the, invariable plural only) People who are old; old beings; the older generation, taken as a group.

old.en: when he was an eight-year-old ; a 62-year-old should
old.definition: (in combination) One of a specified age.

old.en: I had to sneak out to meet my girlfriend and tell the olds I was going to the library.
an old abandoned building;  an old friend
old.definition: Of an object, concept, relationship, etc., having existed for a relatively long period of time.

old.en: I find that an old toothbrush is good to clean the keyboard with.
old.definition: Having been used and thus no longer new or unused.

old.definition: Having existed or lived for the specified time.

old.en: Your constant pestering is getting old.
We're having a good old time. My next car will be a big old SUV.  My wife makes the best little old apple pie in Texas.
old.definition: A grammatical intensifier, often used in describing something positive. (Mostly in idioms like good old, big old and little old, any old and some old.)

on.en: Can you on the light?

on.definition: In the state of being active, functioning or operating.

on.definition: (usually negative) Acceptable, appropriate.

on.definition: So as to cover or be fitted.

on.definition: In continuation, at length.

on.definition: (obsolete in the US) Later.

on.en: The lighthouse that you can see is on the mainland.

on.definition: Supported by (the specified part of itself).

on.definition: So as to impart force to.

on.definition: Because of, or due to.

once.en: I have only once eaten pizza.

once.definition: (temporal location) Formerly; during some period in the past.

one.en: I knew as soon I met him that John was the one for me and we were married within a month.

one.en: Any one of the boys.  The big one looks good.  I want the green one.  A good driver is one who drives carefully.

one.definition: (impersonal pronoun, sometimes with "the") The first mentioned of two things or people, as opposed to the other.

one.en: One’s guilt may trouble one, but it is best not to let oneself be troubled by things which cannot be changed.  One shouldn’t be too quick to judge.
one.definition: (indefinite personal pronoun) Any person (applying to people in general).

online.en: I prefer to read online newspapers.
online.definition: Available over, or delivered from, the Internet.

online.en: I'll be online tonight, so I'll be able to reply to your email.
Press the F1 key to access the online help.
online.definition: Available on a computer system, even if not networked.

only.en: He is the only doctor for miles.

only.definition: Without sibling; without a sibling of the same gender.

only.en: My heart is hers, and hers only.
only.definition: Without others or anything further; exclusively.

only.en: If there were only one more ticket!
She's only gone and run off with the milkman!

only.en: I helped him out only for him to betray me.

only.en: You're welcome to borrow my bicycle, only please take care of it.

onto.en: Considered as a function on the real numbers, the exponential function is not onto.
onto.definition: (of a function) Assuming each of the values in its codomain; having its range equal to its codomain.

onto.en: My cat just jumped onto the keyboard.
The thought-police were onto my plans of world domination.
The exponential function maps the set of real numbers onto the set of positive real numbers.
onto.definition: Being an onto function with a codomain of (see below).

open.en: an open hand; an open flower

open.en: Banks are not open on bank holidays.

open.en: I am open to new ideas.

open.en: an open question

open.en: an open winter

open.en: Turn the doorknob to open the door.

open.en: He opened a path through the undergrowth.

open.en: the Australian Open
open.definition: A sports event in which anybody can compete

opening.en: Turn the doorknob to open the door.
opening.definition: To make something accessible or allow for passage by moving from a shut position.

opening.en: He opened a path through the undergrowth.
opening.definition: To make (an open space, etc.) by clearing away an obstacle or obstacles, in order to allow for passage, access, or visibility.

opening.en: I don't want to open that subject.
to open a case in court, or a meeting

opening.definition: To spread; to expand into an open or loose position.

opening.en: I will open the shop an hour early tomorrow.
opening.definition: To make accessible to customers or clients.

opening.en: Vermont will open elk hunting season next week.
The door opened all by itself.
After the first two players fold, Julie opens for $5.
opening.definition: To bet before any other player has in a particular betting round in a game of poker.

opening.en: Jeff opens his hand revealing a straight flush.
He remembered fondly the Christmas morning opening of presents.
opening.definition: An act or instance of making or becoming open.

opening.en: A salamander darted out of an opening in the rocks.
Their opening of the concert with Brass in Pocket always fires up the crowd.
opening.definition: An act or instance of beginning.

opening.definition: A vacant position, especially in an array.

opening.en: The opening theme of Beethoven's Fifth Symphony is, perhaps, the most recognizable in all of European art music.

operate.definition: To put into, or to continue in, operation or activity; to work.

operation.en: The Katrina relief operation was considered botched.

She had an operation to remove her appendix.

operator.en: In the sentence "What did Bill say he wants to buy?", "what" is an operator, binding a phonetically empty variable.

opinion.en: Every man is a fool in some man's opinion.

opposite.en: She saw him walking on the opposite side of the road.

race.definition: A category or species of something that has emerged or evolved from an older one (with an implied parallel to animal breeding or evolutionary science).

radio.en: I think the boat is sinking; we'd better radio for help. / I radioed him already. / Radio the coordinates this time. / OK. I radioed them the coordinates.
radio.definition: To use two-way radio to transmit (a message) (to another radio or other radio operator).

rage.en: Miniskirts were all the rage back then.

rail.en: We travelled to the seaside by rail.
rail.definition: A railroad; a railway, as a means of transportation.

rail.en: We're experimenting with ads in the right-hand rail.
