rain.en: The rains came late that year.
rain.definition: Condensed water falling from a cloud.

rain.en: A rain of mortar fire fell on our trenches.
rain.definition: An instance of particles or larger pieces of matter moving or falling through air.
To have rain fall from the sky.
To fall as or like rain.

rain.en: The boxer rained punches on his opponent's head.

raise.en: The boss gave me a raise.
raise.definition: An increase in wages or salary; a rise (UK).

raise.en: to raise your hand if you want to say something; to raise your walking stick to defend yourself
raise.definition: (physical) To cause to rise; to lift or elevate.

raise.en: We need to raise the motivation level in the company.

raise.definition: To establish contact with (e.g., by telephone or radio).

raise.en: John bet, and Julie raised, requiring John to put in more money.

raise.en: Two raised to the fifth power equals 32.

range.en: We sell a wide range of cars.

range.definition: The distance from a person or sensor to an object, target, emanation, or event.

range.en: Jones has good range for a big man.
range.definition: The defensive area that a player can cover.

range.en: std::for_each  calls the given function on each value in the input range.
range.definition: A sequential list of values specified by an iterator.

range.en: By playing in comedies as well as in dramas he has proved his acting range.
range.definition: The variety of roles that an actor can play in a satisfactory way.

range.en: The variable x ranges over all real values from 0 to 10.
range.definition: (followed by over) Of a variable, to be able to take any of the values in a specified range.

range.definition: To form a line or a row.

range.en: The peba ranges from Texas to Paraguay.
range.definition: To be native to, or live in, a certain district or region.

rank.definition: Strong of its kind or in character; unmitigated; virulent; thorough; utter (used of negative things).
Strong in growth; growing with vigour or rapidity, hence, coarse or gross.
Causing strong growth; producing luxuriantly; rich and fertile.

rank.en: Your gym clothes are rank, bro – when'd you last wash 'em?
rank.definition: Having a very strong and bad taste or odor.

rank.en: I am a rank amateur as a wordsmith.
rank.definition: Complete, used as an intensifier (usually negative, referring to incompetence).

rank.en: The front rank kneeled to reload while the second rank fired over their heads.
rank.definition: A row of people or things organized in a grid pattern, often soldiers [the corresponding term for the perpendicular columns in such a pattern is "file"].

rank.en: Based on your test scores, you have a rank of 23.
rank.definition: One's position in a list sorted by a shared property such as physical location, population, or quality

rank.en: He rose up through the ranks of the company, from mailroom clerk to CEO.
rank.definition: A hierarchical level in an organization such as the military

rank.en: Phylum is the taxonomic rank below kingdom and above class.
rank.definition: A level in a scientific taxonomy system

rank.en: Their defense ranked third in the league.

rapidly.en: She packed her case rapidly and hurried out.
rapidly.definition: With speed; in a rapid manner.

rare.en: Black pearls are very rare and therefore very valuable.

rarely.en: Rarely do you ever find an eagle this far up the river.
rarely.definition: Not occurring at a regular interval; seldom; not often.

rat.en: What a rat, leaving us stranded here!
rat.definition: A person who is known for betrayal; a scoundrel; a quisling.

rat.en: He loved hockey and was a devoted rink rat.
rat.definition: A person who routinely spends time at a particular location.

rat.en: He is going to rat us out!

rat.en: He ratted a vertical line on his face with a pocket knife.

rate.en: At the height of his powers, he was producing pictures at the rate of four a year.
rate.definition: The proportional relationship between one amount, value etc. and another.

rate.en: The car was speeding down here at a hell of a rate.

rate.definition: The relative speed of change or progress.

rate.en: He asked quite a rate to take me to the airport.
rate.definition: The price of (an individual) thing; cost.

rather.en: I'd like this one rather than the other one.

raw.definition: (of materials, products, etc.) Not treated or processed; in a natural state, unrefined, unprocessed.

(of an emotion, personality, etc.) Unmasked, undisguised, strongly expressed

recommend.en: A city that has much to recommend it.

record.en: The person had a record of the interview so she could review her notes.

record.en: We have no record of you making this payment to us.

record.en: The heat and humidity were both new records.

recover.definition: To salvage, to extricate, to rescue (a thing or person)

recover.definition: To replenish to, resume (a good state of mind or body).

recover.definition: To obtain a positive judgement; to win in a lawsuit.

recover.en: to recover damages in trespass; to recover debt and costs in a legal action or that is owing

recover.definition: To get better, to regain health or prosperity

recover.en: Spinning round, he caught a stone with his ankle, but recovered quickly before turning to face me.

recruit.en: Go to the country to recruit.

reduce.en: to reduce weight, speed, heat, expenses, price, personnel etc.

reduce.en: to reduce a province or a fort

reflect.definition: To mirror, or show the image of something.

reflect.en: His image reflected from the shop window as he walked past.

region.definition: Any considerable and connected part of a space or surface; specifically, a tract of land or sea of considerable but indefinite extent; a country; a district; in a broad sense, a place without special reference to location or extent but viewed as an entity for geographical, social or cultural reasons.

regular.en: Maintaining a high-fibre diet keeps you regular.
regular.definition: Having bowel movements or menstrual periods at constant intervals in the expected way.

regular.en: a regular genius; a regular John Bull
regular.definition: Exemplary; excellent example of; utter, downright.

reinforce.definition: To strengthen, especially by addition or augmentation.

relative.en: Why do my relatives always talk about sex?

relatively.definition: Proportionally, in relation to some larger scale thing.

relax.en: to relax a rope or cord

religion.definition: The way of life committed to by monks and nuns.

religious.en: I'm a religious fan of college basketball.
religious.definition: Highly dedicated, as one would be to a religion.

remain.en: There was no food in the house, so I had to remain hungry.
remain.definition: To continue unchanged in place, form, or condition, or undiminished in quantity; to abide; to stay; to endure; to last.

remaining.en: There was no food in the house, so I had to remain hungry.
remaining.definition: To continue unchanged in place, form, or condition, or undiminished in quantity; to abide; to stay; to endure; to last.

remaining.en: The light remained red for two full minutes.
remaining.definition: To continue in a state of being.

remote.en: A remote operator may control the vehicle with a wireless handset.

The act or process of solving; solution.

rich.en: a rich dish; rich cream or soup; rich pastry

shadow.definition: That which looms as though a shadow.

shadow.en: He did not give even a shadow of respect to the professor.

shall.en: Let us examine that, shall we?

shell.definition: An operating system software user interface, whose primary purpose is to launch other programs and control their interactions; the user's command interpreter. Shell is a way to separate the internal complexity of the implementation of the command from the user. The internals can change while the user experience/interface remains the same.

short.en: 38 short suits fit me right off the rack.

shortly.en: Ideas are generally expressed more shortly in verse than in prose

shot.definition: A measure of alcohol, usually spirits, as taken either from a shot-glass or directly from the bottle, equivalent to about 44 milliliters; 1.5 ounces. ("pony shot"= 30 milliliters; 1 fluid ounce)

shot.en: We got a good shot of the hummingbirds mating.
shot.definition: A single snapshot or an unbroken sequence of photographic film exposures, or the digital equivalent; an unedited sequence of frames.

shot.en: I went to the doctor to get a shot for malaria.

shot.definition: A home run that scores one, two, or three runs (a four run home run is usually referred to as a grand slam).

shot.en: The rear axle will have to be replaced. It’s shot.

shot.definition: (of material, especially silk) Woven from warp and weft strands of different colours, resulting in an iridescent appearance.

shot.en: I have to go to bed now; I’m shot.
This man is wolf shot from seeing too many horror movies with wolves in them, so much so that even the mention of the word “wolf” makes him run in terror and that need committed to the insane asylum.
shot.definition: Scarred silly or crazy of something or someone usually due to a traumatic experience with said fear.
A charge to be paid, a scot or shout.

should.en: I hope that we shall win the game.
should.definition: (modal, auxiliary verb, defective) Used before a verb to indicate the simple future tense in the first person singular or plural.

should.en: (determination): You shall go to the ball!
should.definition: Used similarly to indicate determination or obligation in the second and third persons singular or plural.

should.en: Let us examine that, shall we?
should.definition: Used in questions with the first person singular or plural to suggest a possible future action.

should.en: What do I think? What should I do?
should.definition: (auxiliary) Be obliged to; have an obligation to; indicates that the subject of the sentence has some obligation to execute the sentence predicate or that the speaker has some strong advice but has no authority to enforce it.

should.en: I should exercise more often, but I'm too lazy.
should.definition: (auxiliary) ought to; speaker's opinion, or advice that an action is correct, beneficial, or desirable.

should.en: When you press this button, the pilot flame should ignite.
should.definition: (auxiliary) Will be likely to (become or do something); indicates a degree of possibility or probability that the subject of the sentence is likely to execute the sentence predicate.

should.en: If I should be late, go without me.
should.definition: (auxiliary, subjunctive) Used as a variant of the present subjunctive.

should.en: I should be lucky if I were you.
should.definition: (auxiliary) A variant of would when used with first person subjects.

shout.en: Next up the new single from Beyoncé, but first a shout to Barry Bloggins and his wife Belinda...
shout.definition: A greeting, name-check or other mention, for example on a radio or TV programme.

shout.en: They shouted his name to get his attention.
shout.definition: To utter with a shout; to cry; to shout out

shout.en: He′s shouting us all to the opening night of the play.
shout.definition: To pay for food, drink or entertainment for others.

shout.en: Please don't shout in the chat room.
shout.definition: To post a text message (for example, email) in upper case, regarded as the electronic messaging equivalent of oral shouting.

show.en: I'm taking the kids to the show on Tuesday.

The dog sounds ferocious but it's all show.
show.definition: Mere display or pomp with no substance. (Usually seen in the phrases "all show" and "for show".)

show.en: He played AA ball for years, but never made it to the show.
All he had to show for four years of attendance at college was a framed piece of paper.
show.definition: To display, to have somebody see (something).

show.en: Could you please show him on his way. He has overstayed his welcome.

show.definition: To be visible; to be seen; to appear.

show.en: We waited for an hour, but they never showed.
show.definition: To put in an appearance; show up.

show.en: In the third race: Aces Up won, paying eight dollars; Blarney Stone placed, paying three dollars; and Cinnamon showed, paying five dollars.
show.definition: (racing) To finish third, especially of horses or dogs.

shower.en: Today there will be frequent showers and some sunny spells.
shower.definition: A brief fall of precipitation (spell of rain, or a similar fall of snow, sleet, or cascade).

shower.en: I′m going to have a shower.
shower.definition: An instance of using of this device in order to bathe oneself.

shower.en: a shower of sparks;  a meteor shower;  a Gatorade shower
shower.definition: A quantity of something that has characteristics of a rain shower.

shower.en: Her church group has planned an adoption shower.
shower.definition: A party associated with a significant event in a person's life, at which the person usually receives gifts.

shower.en: That calf is going to be a great shower at the fair this year.
shower.definition: Some object or activity that is shown in a contest

shut.en: to cut grass or hair close

shut.definition: Adhering strictly to a standard or original; exact.

shut.en: The patient was kept under close observation.
shut.definition: Accurate; careful; precise; also, attentive; undeviating; strict.
The act or time of shutting; close.
To close, to stop from being open.

shut.en: If you wait too long, the automatic door will shut.
shut.definition: To close, to stop being open.

shut.en: The pharmacy is shut on Sunday.
shut.definition: To close a business temporarily, or (of a business) to be closed.

shut.en: I shut the cat in the kitchen before going out.
shut.definition: To confine in an enclosed area.

shut.en: He's just shut his finger in the door.
shut.definition: To catch or snag in the act of shutting something.

shut.en: A shut door barred our way into the house.

sick.en: We have to cure the sick.
sick.definition: Sick people in general as a group.

I woke up at 4 am and sicked on the floor.
She was sick all day with the flu.

sick.definition: Tired of or annoyed by something.

sick.en: Dude, this car's got a sick subwoofer!
sick building syndrome; my car is looking pretty sick; my job prospects are pretty sick
He sicced his dog on me!
sick.definition: To incite an attack by, especially a dog or dogs.

side.definition: A bounding straight edge of a two-dimensional shape.
A flat surface of a three-dimensional object; a face.

side.en: Which side of the tray shall I put it on?  The patient was bleeding on the right side.
side.definition: One half (left or right, top or bottom, front or back, etc.) of something or someone.

side.en: Meet me on the north side of the monument.
side.definition: A region in a specified position with respect to something.

side.definition: The portion of the human torso usually covered by the arms when they are not raised; the areas on the left and right between the belly or chest and the back.

One possible aspect of a concept, person or thing.
One set of competitors in a game.

side.en: In the second world war, the Italians were on the side of the Germans.
side.definition: A group having a particular allegiance in a conflict or competition.

side.en: He had to put a bit of side on to hit the pink ball.
I just want to see what's on the other side — James said there was a good film on tonight.

side.definition: A dish that accompanies the main course; a side dish.

side.definition: A line of descent traced through one parent as distinguished from that traced through another.

side.definition: The batters faced in an inning by a particular pitcher

side.definition: To ally oneself, be in an alliance, usually with "with" or rarely "in with"

sight.en: He is losing his sight and now can barely read.
sight.definition: (in the singular) The ability to see.
The act of seeing; perception of objects by the eye; view.

sight.en: We went to London and saw all the sights – Buckingham Palace, Tower Bridge, and so on.

sight.en: This is a darn sight better than what I'm used to at home!

sight.en: In their sight it was harmless.
to sight land from a ship
to sight a rifle or a cannon
sight.definition: To apply sights to; to adjust the sights of; also, to give the proper elevation and direction to by means of a sight.

sign.en: I gave them a thumbs-up sign.
sign.definition: (sometimes also used uncountably) A visible indication.

sign.en: The hunters found deer sign at the end of the trail.
sign.definition: Physical evidence left by an animal.

sign.en: I missed the sign at the corner so I took the wrong turn.

signal.en: My mobile phone can't get a signal in the railway station.

signal.definition: To communicate with (a person or system) by a signal.

signal.en: a signal exploit; a signal service; a signal act of benevolence

With the sound turned off; usually on silent or in silent mode.

silent.en: The Magnavox Odyssey was a silent console.
a silent voter; a silent partner

silent.definition: Of an edit or change to a text, not explicitly acknowledged.

silk.en: The thread made of silk was barely visible.
silk.definition: A fine fiber excreted by the silkworm or other arthropod (such as a spider).

silver.en: to silver a pin;  to silver a glass mirror plate with an amalgam of tin and mercury

Having the clear, musical tone of silver; soft and clear in sound.

similarly.definition: (manner) In a like style or manner.

simply.en: I was simply asking a question.

since.definition: From a specified time in the past.

single.definition: Not married or (in modern times) not involved in a romantic relationship without being married or not dating anyone exclusively.

sink.en: Jones has a two-seamer with heavy sink.

sir.en: Excuse me, sir, do you know the way to the art museum?

sir.en:
Sir, yes, sir!
Don't you sir me, private! I work for a living!

slightly.en: He was slightly built, but tall.

slightly.definition: (degree) To a small extent or degree.

slim.definition: A type of cigarette substantially longer and thinner than normal cigarettes.

small.definition: Not large or big; insignificant; few in number.

small.en: Remember when the children were small?

smart.en: After being hit with a pitch, the batter exclaimed "Ouch, my arm smarts!"

smart.en: You look smart in that business suit.

smart.en: He raised his voice, and it hurt her feelings right smart.

smell.en: I love the smell of fresh bread.

Followed by like or of if descriptive: to have a particular smell, whether good or bad.
(without a modifier) To smell bad; to stink.
To have a particular tincture or smack of any quality; to savour.

smoke.en: I'm going out for a smoke.
smoke.definition: (never plural) An instance of smoking a cigarette, cigar, etc.; the duration of this act.

To inhale and exhale tobacco smoke.

snow.en: We have had several heavy snows this year.

To have snow fall from the sky.

so.en: That is so.  You are responsible for this, is that not so?
It was so hot outside that all the plants died.  He was so good, they hired him on the spot.
I need a piece of cloth so long. [= this long]
Place the napkin on the table just so. If that's what you mean, then say so; (or do so).

so.definition: In the same manner or to the same extent as aforementioned; also.

so.en: so far as;  so long as;  so much as
so.definition: With as To such an extent or degree; as.

so.en: Eat your broccoli so you can have dessert.

Used after a pause for thought to introduce a new topic, question or story.

social.en: They organized a social at the dance club to get people to know each other.

social.en: James is a very social guy; he knows lots of people.

society.en: This society has been known for centuries for its colorful clothing and tight-knit family structure.

whenever.en: Whenever will I find time to finish that jigsaw?
whenever.definition: (interrogative) When ever: emphatic form of when.

whenever.en: Tomorrow I'll get up whenever the sun rises.
whenever.definition: At the (single) time that, no matter when.

whenever.en: Whenever he has a pair of aces, his eyelids twitch.

where.en: A good article will cover the who, the what, the when, the where, the why and the how.
where.definition: The place in which something happens.

where.en: He asked where I grew up.
where.definition: Interrogative adverb, used in either a direct or indirect question: at what place; to what place; what place.

where.en: Where would we be without our parents?
That is the place where we first met.

Where Susy has trouble coloring inside the lines, Johnny has already mastered shading.

where.en: He is looking for a house where he can have a complete office.
where.definition: At or in which place or situation.

where.en: The snowbirds travel where it is warm.
Their job is to go where they are called.

where.definition: In a position, case, etc., in which.

whereas.en: He came first in the race, whereas his brother came last.
whereas.definition: In contrast; whilst on the contrary; although.

wherever.en: Wherever have you been all my life?

wherever.en: Add quotations wherever they are needed.

whether.en: He chose the correct answer, but I don't know whether it was by luck or (whether it was) by skill.
whether.definition: Used to introduce an indirect interrogative question that consists of multiple alternative possibilities (usually with correlative or).

whether.en: Do you know whether he's coming?
whether.definition: Without a correlative, used to introduce a simple indirect question.

whether.en: He's coming, whether you like it or not.
whether.definition: Used to introduce a disjunctive adverbial clause which qualifies the main clause of the sentence (with correlative or).

which.en: By now, you must surely know which is which.
which.definition: (interrogative) What one or ones (of those mentioned or implied).

which.en: He had to leave, which was very difficult.
which.definition: (relative) Introduces a relative clause giving further information about something previously mentioned.

while.en: He lectured for quite a long while.
while.definition: An uncertain duration of time, a period of time.

while.en: He was sleeping while I was singing.

whisper.en: There are whispers of rebellion all around.

whisper.definition: A faint trace or hint (of something).

white.en: Write in black ink on white paper.
white.definition: Bright and colourless; reflecting equal quantities of all frequencies of visible light.

white.en: The white pieces in this set are in fact made of light green glass.
white.definition: The standard denomination of the playing pieces of a board game deemed to belong to the white set, no matter what the actual colour.

who.en: I don't know who it is. (indirect question)

whole.en: This variety of fascinating details didn't fall together into an enjoyable, coherent whole.

whom.en: He's a person with whom I work. (defining)

why.en: A good article will cover the who, the what, the when, the where, the why and the how.

will.definition: (now uncommon or literary) To wish, desire (something).

decent.en: Are you decent? May I come in?

decisive.definition: Having the power or quality of deciding a question or controversy; putting an end to contest or controversy; final; conclusive.

decree.en: A court decrees a restoration of property.

dedicate.en: dedicated their money to scientific research.

deem.definition: (ditransitive) To hold in belief or estimation; to adjudge as a conclusion; to regard as being; to evaluate according to one's beliefs; to account.

default.en: He failed to make payments on time and is now in default.

default.en: If you don't specify a number of items, it defaults to 1.
default.definition: To assume a value when none was given; to presume a tentative value or standard.

defect.en: a defect in the ear or eye; a defect in timber or iron; a defect of memory or judgment

defy.en: to defy an enemy;   to defy the power of a magistrate;   to defy the arguments of an opponent;   to defy public opinion
defy.definition: To challenge (someone) or brave (a hazard or opposition).

denounce.en: to denounce someone as a swindler, or as a coward
denounce.definition: To criticize or speak out against (someone or something); to point out as deserving of reprehension, etc.; to openly accuse or condemn in a threatening manner; to invoke censure upon; to stigmatize; to blame.

denounce.definition: To make a formal or public accusation against; to inform against; to accuse.

descend.en: The rain descended, and the floods came.

desirable.en: There are plenty of desirables on display in the window.

desolate.en: a desolate isle; a desolate wilderness; a desolate house

diplomatic.en: Albania immediately severed diplomatic relations with Zimbabwe.

diplomatic.en: Thoughtful corrections can be diplomatic as well as instructional.

diplomatic.en: Whereas a diplomatic edition uses as its base text a single, "best" manuscript, to which other textual evidence is collated and organized into an apparatus, a critical text of the LXX/OG [= Septuagint or Old Greek] may be described as a collection of the oldest recoverable texts, carefully restored book by book (or section by section), aiming at achieving the closest approximation to the original translations (from Hebrew or Aramaic) or compositions (in Greek), systematically reconstructed from the widest array of relevant textual data (including controlled conjecture).  The International Organization for Septuagint and Cognate Studies, Critical Editions of Septuagint/Old Greek Texts.
diplomatic.definition: Describing a publication of a text which follows a single basic manuscript, but with variants in other manuscripts noted in the critical apparatus

discern.en: He was too young to discern right from wrong.
discern.definition: To distinguish something as being different from something else; to differentiate.

discharge.definition: To release legally from confinement; to set at liberty.

discharge.en: to discharge the colour from a dyed fabric in order to form light figures on a dark background
discharge.definition: To bleach out or to remove or efface, as by a chemical process.

diverse.en: Idris Elba was a diverse hire for the franchise

elbow.en: the sides of windows, where the jamb makes an elbow with the window back

encyclopedia.en: His life's work was a four-volume encyclopedia of aviation topics.

endurance.en: He has great endurance: he ran a marathon and then cycled home.

erect.en: The penis should be fully erect before commencing copulation.

erroneous.en: His answer to the sum was erroneous.

esteem.en: We hold her in high esteem.

esteem.en: The Earth, which I esteem unable to reflect the rays of the Sun.

ethnic.en: There are many ethnic Indonesians in the Netherlands

exceed.en: The company's 2005 revenue exceeds that of 2004.

exceed.definition: To go beyond (some limit); to surpass; to be longer than.

exception.en: That rule is usually true, but there are a few exceptions.

exclusive.en: The teacher's pet commands the teacher's exclusive attention.

heal.en: This bandage will heal your cut.

To become better or healthy again.
To reconcile, as a breach or difference; to make whole; to free from guilt.

heap.en: a heap of earth or stones

heritage.definition: Having a certain background, such as growing up with a second language.

horizontal.en: 1667: As when the Sun new ris'n / Looks through the Horizontal misty Air — John Milton, Paradise Lost, Book 1, ll. 594-5

parameter.en: Roughly, a tuple of arguments could be thought of as a vector, whereas a tuple of parameters could be thought of as a covector (i.e., linear functional). When a function is called, a parameter tuple becomes "bound" to an argument tuple, allowing the function instance itself to be computed to yield a return value. This would be roughly analogous to applying a covector to a vector (by taking their dot product (or, rather, matrix-product of row vector and column vector)) to obtain a scalar.

perpetual.en: By means of artificial hybridization, practised for a series of years, he has succeeded in producing a race of carnations which are perpetual bloomers.

persecute.en: He who persecutes one will persecute all.
persecute.definition: To pursue in a manner to injure, grieve, or afflict; to beset with cruelty or malignity; to harass; especially, to afflict, harass, punish, or put to death for one's race, sexual identity, adherence to a particular religious creed, or mode of worship.

persistence.en: You've got to admire his persistence. He's asked her out every day for a month even though she keeps turning him down.
Once written to a disk file, the data has persistence: it will still be there tomorrow when we run the next program.
persistence.definition: Of data, the property of continuing to exist after the termination of the program.

petition.en: The villagers petitioned the council to demolish the dangerous building.
petition.definition: To make a request to, commonly in written form.

physics.en: Newtonian physics was extended by Einstein to explain the effects of travelling near the speed of light; quantum physics extends it to account for the behaviour of atoms.
physics.definition: The branch of science concerned with the study of the properties and interactions of space, time, matter and energy.

physics.en: The physics of car crashes would not let Tom Cruise walk away like that.
physics.definition: The physical aspects of a phenomenon or a system, especially those studied scientifically.

pillar.en: He's a pillar of the community.
pillar.definition: An essential part of something that provides support.

plague.en: Ten Biblical plagues over Egypt, ranging from locusts to the death of the crown prince, finally forced Pharaoh to let Moses's people go.
plague.definition: A widespread affliction, calamity or destructive influx, especially when seen as divine retribution.

plague.en: Bart is an utter plague; his pranks never cease.
plague.definition: A grave nuisance, whatever greatly irritates.

plague.en: Natural catastrophes plagued the colonists till they abandoned the pestilent marshland.
plague.definition: To afflict with a disease or other calamity.

plea.definition: An appeal, petition, urgent prayer or entreaty.

plead.en: The defendant has decided to plead not guilty.
plead.definition: To present (an argument or a plea), especially in a legal case.

plead.en: He pleaded with me not to leave the house.
Not wishing to attend the banquet, I pleaded illness.
plead.definition: To offer by way of excuse.

plunge.definition: A dive, leap, rush, or pitch into (into water)

plunge.en: to plunge the body into water
plunge.definition: To thrust into liquid, or into any penetrable substance; to immerse.

plunge.en: to plunge a dagger into the breast;   to plunge a nation into war
plunge.definition: To cast, stab or throw into some thing, state, condition or action.
To dive, leap or rush (into water or some liquid); to submerge oneself.

plunge.en: to plunge into debt;   to plunge into controversy
plunge.definition: To fall or rush headlong into some thing, action, state or condition.

polish.en: A good silver polish will remove tarnish easily.
The floor was waxed to a high polish.
The lecturer showed a lot of polish at his last talk.
polish.definition: Refinement; cleanliness in performance or presentation.

polish.en: He polished up the chrome until it gleamed.
polish.definition: To shine; to make a surface very smooth or shiny by rubbing, cleaning, or grinding.

polish.en: The band has polished its performance since the last concert.

polish.definition: To become smooth, as from friction; to receive a gloss; to take a smooth and glossy surface.

poll.en: The student council had a poll to see what people want served in the cafeteria.

poll.definition: A polling place (usually as plural, polling places)

poll.en: He polled a hundred votes more than his opponent.
poll.definition: To register or deposit, as a vote; to elicit or call forth, as votes or voters.

poll.en: to poll the hair; to poll wool; to poll grass
poll.definition: To cut off; to remove by clipping, shearing, etc.; to mow or crop.
To remove the top or end of; to clip; to lop.

poll.en: The network hub polled the department's computers to determine which ones could still respond.
poll.definition: (communication) To (repeatedly) request the status of something (such as a computer or printer on a network).
To cut or shave smooth or even; to cut in a straight line without indentation.
(of kinds of livestock which typically have horns) Bred without horns, and thus hornless.

ponder.en: I lit my pipe and had a ponder about it, but reached no definite conclusion.
I have spent days pondering the meaning of life.
ponder.definition: To consider (something) carefully and thoroughly; to chew over, mull over

populace.en: The populace despised their ignorant leader.
populace.definition: The common people of a nation.

portfolio.en: I would like to introduce you to our portfolio of services.

portray.en: I will portray a king on horseback.
portray.definition: To paint or draw the likeness of.

portray.en: For my next movie, I will be portraying Shakespeare.
portray.definition: To play a role; to depict a character, person, situation, or event.

posture.en: If you're finished posturing in front of the mirror, can I use the bathroom now?
posture.definition: To put one's body into a posture or series of postures, especially hoping that one will be noticed and admired

posture.en: The politicians couldn't really care less about the issue: they're just posturing for the media.
posture.definition: To pretend to have an opinion or a conviction

posture.en: to posture oneself; to posture a model
posture.definition: To place in a particular position or attitude; to pose.

pragmatic.en: The sturdy furniture in the student lounge was pragmatic, but unattractive.
pragmatic.definition: Practical, concerned with making decisions and actions that are useful in practice, not just theory.

preach.en: A learned local Muslim used to preach in the small mosque every Friday.

precaution.en: his life was saved by precaution
precaution.definition: Previous caution or care; caution previously employed to prevent misfortune or to secure good

precaution.en: to take precautions against risks of accident
precaution.definition: A measure taken beforehand to ward off evil or secure good or success; a precautionary act.

precede.definition: To go before, go in front of.

precipitate.definition: To make something happen suddenly and quickly.

precipitate.en: we were precipitated into a conflict
precipitate.definition: To send violently into a certain state or condition.

precipitate.en: Adding the acid will cause the salt to precipitate.
precipitate.definition: To come out of a liquid solution into solid form.

precipitate.en: It will precipitate tomorrow, but we don't know whether as rain or snow.
precipitate.definition: To have water in the air fall to the ground, for example as rain, snow, sleet, or hail; be deposited as condensed droplets.

precipitate.en: The king was too precipitate in declaring war.
precipitate.definition: Moving with excessive speed or haste.

preface.en: The book included a brief preface by a leading expert in the field.
preface.definition: The beginning or introductory portion that comes before the main text of a document or book.

preface.en: Let me preface this by saying that I don't know him that well.
preface.definition: To introduce or make a comment before (the main point).

preliminary.en: These are just the preliminary results.
preliminary.definition: In preparation for the main matter; initial, introductory, preparatory.

prescribe.definition: To order (a drug or medical device) for use by a particular patient (under licensed authority).

prescribe.en: The property meets the criteria prescribed by the regulations.
prescribe.definition: To specify by writing as a required procedure or ritual; to lay down authoritatively as a guide, direction, or rule of action.

prescription.en: "Jurisdiction to prescribe" is a state's authority to make its laws applicable to certain persons or activities. -- Richard G. Alexander, Iran and Libya Sanctions Act of 1996: Congress exceeds its jurisdiction to prescribe law. Washington and Lee Law Review, 1997.
prescription.definition: The act of prescribing a rule, law, etc..

prescription.en: The surgeon wrote a prescription for a pain killer and physical therapy.
prescription.definition: A written order, as by a physician or nurse practitioner, for the administration of a medicine or other intervention. See also scrip.

prescription.en: The pharmacist gave her a bottle containing her prescription.
prescription.definition: The prescription medicine or intervention so prescribed.

prescription.en: The optician followed the optometrist's prescription for her new eyeglasses.
prescription.definition: The formal description of the lens geometry needed for spectacles, etc..

prescription.en: Many powerful pain killers are prescription drugs in the U.S.
prescription.definition: (of a drug, etc.) only available with a physician or nurse practitioner's written prescription

prestige.en: Oxford has a university of very high prestige.
prestige.definition: The quality of how good the reputation of something or someone is, how favourably something or someone is regarded.

presumably.en: Presumably, he will attend the opening.

presume.en: I wouldn't presume to tell him how to do his job.
presume.definition: With infinitive object: to be so presumptuous as (to do something) without proper authority or permission.

presume.en: Don't make the decision yourself and presume too much.
presume.definition: To perform, do (something) without authority; to lay claim to without permission.
To assume or suggest to be true (without proof); to take for granted, to suppose.

prevail.en: Red colour prevails in the Canadian flag.
prevail.definition: To be superior in strength, dominance, influence or frequency; to have or gain the advantage over others; to have the upper hand; to outnumber others.

prevail.en: In his day and age, such practices prevailed all over Europe.
prevail.definition: To be current, widespread or predominant; to have currency or prevalence.

prevail.en: I prevailed on him to wait.
prevail.definition: To succeed in persuading or inducing.

prevention.definition: The act of preventing or hindering; obstruction of action, access, or approach; thwarting.

prey.en: The rabbit was eaten by the coyote, so the rabbit is the coyote's prey.
prey.definition: A living thing that is eaten by another living thing.

probe.en: They launched a probe into the cause of the accident.
Insert the probe into the soil and read the temperature.
probe.definition: A small device, especially an electrode, used to explore, investigate or measure something by penetrating or being placed in it.

probe.en: If you probe further, you may discover different reasons.

proceeding.definition: To move, pass, or go forward or onward; to advance; to carry on

proceeding.en: To proceed with a story or argument.
proceeding.definition: To pass from one point, topic, or stage, to another.
To come from; to have as its source or origin.

proceeding.en: a cautious or a violent proceeding
proceeding.definition: A measure or step taken in a course of business; a transaction

procession.en: a procession of mourners; the Lord Mayor's procession
procession.definition: A group of people or things moving along in an orderly, stately, or solemn manner; a train of persons advancing in order; a retinue.

procurement.en: He was responsible for the procurement of materials and supplies.
procurement.definition: The act of procuring or obtaining; obtainment; attainment.

procurement.en: They think it done by her procurement. -Dryden.

profile.en: His fingers traced the profile of the handle.
profile.definition: The outermost shape, view, or edge of an object.

profile.en: The brooch showed the profile of a Victorian woman.
profile.definition: The shape, view, or shadow of a person's head from the side; a side view.

profile.en: Law enforcement assembled a profile of the suspect.
profile.definition: A summary or collection of information, especially about a person

profile.en: I just updated my Facebook profile to show I got engaged.
profile.definition: A specific space or field in which users can provide various types of personal information in software or Internet systems.

profile.en: Choose a handle with a low profile so it does not catch on things.
profile.definition: The amount by which something protrudes.

profile.en: Acting is, by nature, profession in which one must keep a high profile.
What's the thermal profile on that thing?
profile.definition: Character; totality of related characteristics; signature; status (especially in scientific, technical, or military uses).

profound.definition: Intellectually deep; entering far into subjects; reaching to the bottom of a matter, or of a branch of learning; thorough

progressive.definition: Promoting or favoring progress towards improved conditions or new policies, ideas or methods.
Of or relating to progressive education.

prohibit.en: The restaurant prohibits smoking on the patio.
prohibit.definition: To forbid, disallow, or proscribe officially; to make illegal or illicit.

projection.en: The face of the cliff had many projections that were big enough for birds to nest on.
projection.definition: Something which projects, protrudes, juts out, sticks out, or stands out.

propagate.definition: To cause to spread to extend; to impel or continue forward in space

propagate.en: It takes 24 hours for password changes to propagate throughout the system.
propagate.definition: To take effect on all relevant devices in a network.

propagate.en: The server propagates the password file at midnight each day.
propagate.definition: To cause to take effect on all relevant devices in a network.

proposition.en: “'Wiktionary is a good dictionary' is a proposition” is a proposition.
proposition.definition: The content of an assertion that may be taken as being true or false and is considered abstractly without reference to the linguistic sentence that constitutes the assertion; (Aristotelian logic) a predicate of a subject that is denied or affirmed and connected by a copula.

proposition.en: the propositions of Wyclif and Huss
proposition.definition: A statement of religious doctrine; an article of faith; creed.

prose.en: Though known mostly for her prose, she also produced a small body of excellent poems.
prose.definition: Language, particularly written language, not intended as poetry.

prosecute.en: to prosecute a man for trespass, or for a riot
to prosecute a right or a claim in a court of law
prosecute.definition: To seek to obtain by legal process.

prosecute.en: to prosecute a scheme, hope, or claim
prosecute.definition: To pursue something to the end.

prosecution.en: The prosecution of the war fell to Winston Churchill.
prosecution.definition: The act of prosecuting a scheme or endeavor.

prospective.definition: (often plural) A prospective (potential) member, student, employee, date, partner, etc.

prospective.en: Prospective students are those who have already applied to the university, but have yet to be admitted.
prospective.definition: Likely or expected to happen or become.

prospective.en: What some other languages convey with prospective aspect, English conveys with expressions like going to drive the car home.
prospective.definition: (grammar) Indicating grammatically an activity about to begin.

prosperous.en: Trading Babe Ruth was far more prosperous for the Yankees than for the Red Sox.
He was raised in a very prosperous household.
He chose a prosperous lottery number that evening.

prototype.en: The prototype had loose wires and rough edges, but it worked.
prototype.definition: An early sample or model built to test a concept or process.

prototype.en: A robin is a prototype of a bird; a penguin is not.
prototype.definition: An instance of a category or a concept that combines its most representative attributes.

provoke.en: Don't provoke the dog; it may try to bite you.
provoke.definition: To cause someone to become annoyed or angry.

proximity.en: The proximity of the heat source allowed it to be detected by the sensor.
proximity.definition: Closeness; the state of being near as in space, time, or relationship.

punch.en: If she punches me, I'm gonna break her nose.
He punched a hit into shallow left field.
punch.definition: To hit (a ball or similar object) with less than full force.

punch.en: to punch one with the end of a stick or the elbow

quest.en: to rove in quest of game, of a lost child, of property, etc.
quest.definition: The act of seeking, or looking after anything; attempt to find or obtain; search; pursuit.

quotation.en: "Where they burn books, they will also burn people" is a famous quotation from Heinrich Heine.
quotation.definition: A fragment of a human expression that is repeated by somebody else. Most often a quotation is taken from literature or speech, but also scenes from a movie, elements of a painting, a passage of music, etc., may be quoted.

quotation.en: Let's get a quotation for repairing the roof before we decide whether it's worth doing.
quotation.definition: A price that has been quoted for buying or selling.

radar.en: His sensitive radar for hidden alliances keeps him out of trouble.
radar.definition: A superior ability to detect something.

radiate.en: The heat radiates from a stove.
radiate.definition: To come out or proceed in rays or waves.
Radiating from a center; having rays or parts diverging from a center; radiated.

radiation.definition: The shooting forth of anything from a point or surface, like diverging rays of light.

radical.definition: Favoring fundamental change, or change at the root cause of a matter.

radical.en: The spread of the cancer required radical surgery, and the entire organ was removed.
a radical quantity; a radical sign
radical.definition: Relating to a radix or mathematical root.

raid.en: a police raid of a narcotics factory
raid.definition: An attack or invasion for the purpose of making arrests, seizing property, or plundering

raid.en: The police raided the gambling den.
raid.definition: To engage in a raid against.

raid.en: I raided the fridge for snacks.
raid.definition: To indulge oneself by taking from.

rally.definition: A public gathering or mass meeting that is not mainly a protest and is organized to inspire enthusiasm for a cause.
A protest or demonstration for or against something, but often with speeches and often without marching, especially in North America.

random.en: The party was boring. It was full of randoms.
random.definition: An undefined, unknown or unimportant person; a person of no consequence.

random.en: The flip of a fair coin is purely random.
random.definition: Having unpredictable outcomes and, in the ideal case, all outcomes equally probable; resulting from such selection; lacking statistical correlation.

random.en: A toss of loaded dice is still random, though biased.
random.definition: Of or relating to probability distribution.

random.en: A random American off the street couldn't tell the difference.
random.definition: (somewhat colloquial) Representative and undistinguished; typical and average; selected for no particular reason.

random.en: That was a completely random comment.
random.definition: (somewhat colloquial) Apropos of nothing; lacking context; unexpected; having apparent lack of plan, cause or reason.
Characterized by or often saying random things; habitually using non sequiturs.

ratio.en: The schoolchildren ganged up to ratio the school catering over its shamefully undemocratic inclusion of vegetables in meals.
ratio.definition: (social media) To respond to a post or message on social media in a greater number than the number of likes the post receives.

rational.en: The quotient of two rationals is again a rational.
rational.definition: A rational number: a number that can be expressed as the quotient of two integers.
Logically sound; not contradictory or otherwise absurd.
(of a person or personal characteristics) Healthy or balanced intellectually; exhibiting reasonableness.

rational.en: ¾ is a rational number, but √2 is an irrational number.
rational.definition: Of a number, capable of being expressed as the ratio of two integers.

rational.en: A rational table is physical, a written table is neither.

reap.en: to reap a benefit from exertions
reap.definition: To obtain or receive as a reward, in a good or a bad sense.

reap.en: Until a child process is reaped, it may be listed in the process table as a zombie or defunct process.
reap.definition: To terminate a child process that has previously exited, thereby removing it from the process table.

reassure.en: The boy's mother reassured him that there was no monster hiding under the bed.
reassure.definition: To assure anew; to restore confidence to; to free from fear or self-doubt.

rebellion.en: The government is doing its best to stop rebellion in the country.
rebellion.definition: Armed resistance to an established government or ruler.

rebellion.en: Having a tattoo was Mathilda's personal rebellion against her parents.
rebellion.definition: Defiance of authority or control; the act of rebelling.

rebellion.en: The army general led a successful rebellion and became president of the country.
rebellion.definition: An organized, forceful subversion of the law of the land in an attempt to replace it with another form of government.

recipient.en: My e-mail never reached the intended recipient.

reciprocal.definition: The number obtained by dividing 1 by another given number; the result of exchanging the numerator and the denominator of a fraction.
Of a feeling, action or such: mutual, uniformly felt or done by each party towards the other or others; two-way.
Done, given, felt, or owed in return

reckon.en: I reckon he won't try that again.
reckon.definition: To conclude, as by an enumeration and balancing of chances; hence, to think; to suppose; -- followed by an objective clause

reconcile.en: to reconcile people who have quarrelled
reconcile.definition: To restore a friendly relationship; to bring back to harmony.
To make things compatible or consistent.

reconciliation.en: He longed for reconciliation with his estranged father, but too many painful memories kept him from making contact again.
reconciliation.definition: The reestablishment of friendly relations; conciliation or rapprochement.

recur.en: The theme of the prodigal son recurs later in the third act.

recycle.en: Both paper and plastic can be recycled.
recycle.definition: To break down and reuse component materials.

recycle.en: Sulfur recycles in the sulfur cycle.

referendum.en: My father is taking my decision on whether to go to university as a referendum on his performance as a parent, and it's very stressful.
referendum.definition: An action, choice, etc., which is perceived as passing judgment on another matter.

refine.definition: To purify; reduce to a fine, unmixed, or pure state; to free from impurities.
To purify of coarseness, vulgarity, inelegance, etc.; to polish.

refund.en: If the camera is faulty, you can return it to the store where you bought it for a full refund.
If you find this computer for sale anywhere at a lower price, we'll refund you the difference.

regime.definition: A form of government, or the government in power.

regime.en: Heaven will eliminate the tyrannical regimes.
A typical annual water level regime would include a gradual summer drawdown beginning in early May.

reign.en: England prospered under Elizabeth I's reign.
The reign of Victoria was a long one.
reign.definition: The period during which a monarch rules.

reign.en: He reigned in an autocratic manner.
reign.definition: To exercise sovereign power, to rule as a monarch.
To be a dominant quality of a place or situation; to prevail, predominate, rule.

relay.en: Can you relay this message to John?
He had to re-lay the tiles because the cement was too dry.
relay.definition: To lay (for example, flooring or railroad track) again.

reliable.definition: Something or someone reliable or dependable

reliance.en: Your reliance on his expertise may be misplaced.
reliance.definition: The act of relying (on or in someone or something); trust.

reliance.en: The industry is working to phase out its reliance on fossil fuels.
reliance.definition: The condition of being reliant or dependent.

reliance.en: The boat was a poor reliance.
reliance.definition: Anything on which to rely; ground of trust.

remainder.en: My son ate part of his cake and I ate the remainder.
remainder.definition: A part or parts remaining after some has/have been removed.

The amount left over after subtracting the divisor as many times as possible from the dividend without producing a negative result. If (n) (dividend) and d (divisor) are integers, then (n) can always be expressed in the form n = dq + r, where q (quotient) and r (remainder) are also integers and 0 ≤ r < d.
The number left over after a simple subtraction

remainder.en: I got a really good price on this shirt because it was a remainder.
remainder.definition: Excess stock items left unsold and subject to reduction in price.

remainder.en: The bookstore remaindered the unsold copies of that book at the end of summer at a reduced price.
remainder.definition: To mark or declare items left unsold as subject to reduction in price.

remit.en: to remit the performance of an obligation
remit.definition: To refrain from exacting or enforcing.

render.en: A low-resolution render might look blocky.
render.definition: A digital image produced by rendering a model.

render.en: The pianist rendered the Beethoven sonata beautifully.
render.definition: To interpret, give an interpretation or rendition of.

render.en: render a verdict (i.e., deliver a judgment)
They had to render the estate.
render.definition: To make over as a return.
To give; to give back; to deliver.
To transform (a model) into a display on the screen or other media.
To convert waste animal tissue into a usable byproduct.

render.en: Bacon is very fatty when raw; however, most of the fat will render during cooking.
render.definition: To have fat drip off meat from cooking.
To cover a wall with a layer of plaster.

render.en: A rope renders well, that is, passes freely.
render.definition: To pass; to run; said of the passage of a rope through a block, eyelet, etc.

renew.en: I'd like to renew these three books.  Did you know that you can renew online?
renew.definition: To extend a period of loan, especially a library book that is due to be returned.

renowned.en: The movie “Three Stars” is about world-renowned chefs.

rental.en: It's just a rental, so I don't need to take very good care of it.

repay.en: I finally repaid my student loans, just before sending my kids to college.

repertoire.en: The conjurer expanded his repertoire with some new tricks.
repertoire.definition: A list of dramas, operas, pieces, parts, etc., which a company or a person has rehearsed and is prepared to perform or display.

reproduction.en: Unauthorized reproduction of this article is prohibited.
Jim was proud of the Rembrandt reproduction he owned.
reproduction.definition: A copy of something, as in a piece of art; a duplicate.

requisite.en: Please submit the requisite papers before the end of the financial year.

resemble.definition: To be like or similar to (something); to represent as similar.

resent.en: The bride greatly resented being left at the church.
resent.definition: To feel resentment over; to consider as an affront.

resent.en: I didn't get your email. You'll have to resend it.

residential.en: The youth group organises annual residentials.
residential.definition: A trip during which people temporarily live together.

residential.en: They live in a residential neighborhood.
residential.definition: Of or pertaining to a place of personal residence or to a location for such places.

residential.en: There is a residential requirement for obtaining a marriage license here.

resistant.en: The infection is resistant to antibiotics.
resistant.definition: Which is not affected or overcome by a disease, drug, chemical or atmospheric agent, extreme of temperature, etc.

respective.en: They returned to their respective places of abode.
respective.definition: Relating to particular persons or things, each to each; particular; own.
Looking toward; having reference to; relative, not absolute.

respectively.en: Serena Williams and Roger Federer won the women's and men's singles titles, respectively, at the 2010 Australian Open.
respectively.definition: In a relative manner; often used when comparing lists, where the term denotes that the items in the lists correspond to each other in the order they are given.

restoration.en: The restoration of this medieval church involved undoing all the Victorian modifications.
restoration.definition: The process of bringing an object back to its original state; the process of restoring something.

restoration.en: The restoration of the House of Stuart took place a few years after the death of Cromwell.
restoration.definition: The return of a former monarchy or monarch to power, usually after having been forced to step down.

restrain.en: He was restrained by the straight jacket.

restraint.en: Make sure all the restraints are tight.
restraint.definition: Something that restrains, ties, fastens or secures

restraint.en: Try to exercise restraint when talking to your boss.

resume.en: We will resume this discussion tomorrow at nine.
resume.definition: To start (something) again that has been stopped or paused from the point at which it was stopped or paused; continue, carry on.

retail.definition: The sale of goods directly to the consumer, encompassing the storefronts, mail-order, websites, etc., and the corporate mechanisms, branding, advertising, etc. that support them.

retail.en: I never pay retail for clothes.
retail.definition: Retail price; full price; an abbreviated expression, meaning the full suggested price of a particular good or service, before any sale, discount, or other deal.

retail.en: We've shut shown our reseller unit. We're only selling retail now.
retail.definition: Direct to consumers, in retail quantities, or at retail prices.

retort.en: to retort the charge of vanity
retort.definition: To make a remark which reverses an argument upon its originator; to return, as an argument, accusation, censure, or incivility.

retrieve.en: to retrieve one's character or independence; to retrieve a thrown ball
retrieve.definition: To regain or get back something.

retrieve.en: The cook doesn't care what's shot, only what's actually retrieved.
retrieve.definition: To fetch and bring in game.

retrieve.en: Dog breeds called 'retrievers' were selected for retrieving.
retrieve.definition: To fetch and bring in game systematically.

retrieve.en: Most dogs love retrieving, regardless of what object is thrown.
retrieve.definition: To fetch or carry back systematically, notably as a game.

retrospect.en: 1800: Alexander Hamilton, Letter from Alexander Hamilton, Concerning the Public Conduct and Character of John Adams, Esq. President of the United States - To give a correct idea of the circumstances.., it may be useful to retrospect to an early period.
retrospect.definition: To look or refer back to; to reflect on.

revenge.definition: Any form of personal retaliatory action against an individual, institution, or group for some alleged or perceived harm or injustice.

revenge.en: Arsenal revenged its loss to Manchester United last time with a 5–0 drubbing this time.
revenge.definition: To take revenge for (a particular harmful action) or on behalf of (its victim); to avenge.

reverse.en: We believed the Chinese weren't ready for us. In fact, the reverse was true.
to reverse a portion of video footage
reverse.definition: To turn something around so that it faces the opposite direction or runs in the opposite sequence.

reverse.en: to reverse a judgment, sentence, or decree
reverse.definition: To revoke a law, or to change a decision into its opposite.

reverse.en: The mirror showed us a reverse view of the scene.
reverse.definition: Opposite, contrary; going in the opposite direction.
Pertaining to engines, vehicle movement etc. moving in a direction opposite to the usual direction.

revision.definition: A story corrected or expanded by a writer commissioned by the original author.

revision.en: What philosophy needs is to be revised with a more hopeful, engaged inspirational point of view.

taxpayer.en: 1945: I got into the bus full of taxpayers who were giving some money to a taxpayer who had on his taxpayer's stomach a little box which allowed the other taxpayers to continue their taxpayers' journeys. I noticed in this bus a taxpayer with a long taxpayer's neck and whose taxpayer's head bore a taxpayer's felt hat encircled by a plait the like of which no taxpayer ever wore before. Suddenly the said taxpayer peremptorily addressed a nearby taxpayer, complaining bitterly that he was purposely treading on his taxpayer's toes every time other taxpayers got on or off the taxpayers' bus. Then the angry taxpayer went and sat down in a seat for taxpayers which another taxpayer had just vacated. — Raymond Queneau, 'from Polyptotes' in Exercises in Style, 1945 (Eng. 1958)

variable.definition: Something whose value may be dictated or discovered.

variable.en: variable winds or seasons; a variable quantity; a variable resistor
variable.definition: Able to vary or be varied.

variance.en: Depending on the variance of the type constructor, the subtyping relation of the simple types may be either preserved, reversed, or ignored for the respective complex types.

vegetation.en: There were large amounts of vegetation in the forest.

vendor.en: I distributed my application with a vendored copy of Perl so that it wouldn't use the system copies of Perl where it is installed.
vendor.definition: To bundle third-party dependencies with the source code for one's own program.

vendor.en: Strawberry Perl contains vendored copies of some CPAN modules, designed to allow them to run on Windows.
vendor.definition: As the software vendor, to bundle one's own, possibly modified version of dependencies with a standard program.

verbal.definition: Expressly spoken rather than written; oral.

verdict.en: The jury returned a “not guilty” verdict.
verdict.definition: A decision on an issue of fact in a civil or criminal case or an inquest.

verdict.en: a “not out” verdict from the umpire

versatile.en: 2004, "queenchaser1158", Horny Versatile TV/TS Wanted in Phoenix (on newsgroup alt.personals.gay)
versatile.definition: (BDSM) Being a switch; capable of taking either a dominant or a submissive role.

verse.en: Restoration literature is well known for its carefully constructed verse.
verse.definition: A poetic form with regular meter and a fixed rhyme scheme.

verse.en: The restrictions of verse have been steadily relaxed over time.
Note the shift in tone between the first verse and the second.
verse.definition: One of several similar units of a song, consisting of several lines, generally rhymed.

verse.en: He versed us in the finer points of category theory.
verse.definition: To educate about, to teach about.
To oppose, to compete against, especially in a video game.

versus.en: It is the Packers versus the Steelers in the Super Bowl.
Brown v. Board of Education of Topeka, Kans.
versus.definition: Bringing a legal action against, as used in the title of a court case in which the first party indicates the plaintiff (or appellant or the like), and the second indicates the defendant (or respondent or the like).

vertical.en: We offer specialised accounting software targeting various verticals.

virus.en: He caught a virus and had to stay home from school.
virus.definition: A disease caused by these organisms.

vocabulary.en: My Russian vocabulary is very limited.
vocabulary.definition: The collection of words a person knows and uses.

vocabulary.en: The vocabulary of social sciences is often incomprehensible to ordinary people.
vocabulary.definition: The stock of words used in a particular field.

vocabulary.en: The vocabulary of any language is influenced by contacts with other cultures.
vocabulary.definition: The words of a language collectively.

vow.en: The old hermit, up in the mountains, took a vow of silence.
vow.definition: A solemn promise to perform some act, or behave in a specified manner, especially a promise to live and act in accordance with the rules of a religious order.

vow.en: The rebels vowed to continue their fight.
vow.definition: To declare publicly that one has made a vow, usually to show one's determination or to announce an act of retaliation.

vulnerable.en: It's okay to get vulnerable every now and again.
vulnerable.definition: More or most likely to be exposed to the chance of being attacked or harmed, either physically or emotionally.

vulnerable.en: a vulnerable PC with no antivirus software
vulnerable.definition: More likely to be exposed to malicious programs or viruses.

warehouse.en: the warehousing of syndicated TV shows
warehouse.definition: To acquire and then shelve, simply to prevent competitors from acquiring it.

warrant.en: a warrant of authenticity; a warrant for success
warrant.definition: Something that provides assurance or confirmation; a guarantee or proof.

warrant.en: an arrest warrant issued by the court
warrant.definition: A judicial writ authorizing an officer to make a search, seizure, or arrest, or to execute a judgment.

warrant.en: That tree is going to fall, I’ll warrant.
warrant.definition: To guarantee as being true; (colloquially) to believe strongly.

warrant.en: I am warranted to search these premises fully.
warrant.definition: To authorize; to give (someone) sanction or warrant (to do something).

warrant.en: Circumstances arose that warranted the use of lethal force.
warrant.definition: To justify; to give grounds for.

warranty.en: I made sure to check the terms of my warranty for my computer to ensure I was covered in case it broke down.
warranty.definition: A written guarantee, usually over a fixed period, provided to someone who buys a product or item, which states that repairs will be provided free of charge in case of damage or a fault.

weave.en: That rug has a very tight weave.
weave.definition: A type or way of weaving.

weave.en: This loom weaves yarn into sweaters.
weave.definition: To form something by passing lengths or strands of material over and under one another.

weave.en: Spiders weave beautiful but deadly webs.
weave.definition: To spin a cocoon or a web.

weave.en: to weave the plot of a story
weave.definition: To compose creatively and intricately; to fabricate.

weave.en: The drunk weaved into another bar.
weave.definition: To move by turning and twisting.

weave.en: The ambulance weaved its way through the heavy traffic.
weave.definition: To make (a path or way) by winding in and out or from side to side.

whatsoever.en: He gave me no answer whatsoever.

whereby.definition: (interrogative) By what, in which direction; how.

whistle.en: the whistle of the wind in the trees
whistle.definition: Any high-pitched sound similar to the sound made by whistling.
To make a shrill, high-pitched sound by forcing air through the mouth. To produce a whistling sound, restrictions to the flow of air are created using the teeth, tongue and lips.

whistle.en: The stream train whistled as it passed by.
whistle.definition: To make a similar sound by forcing air through a musical instrument or a pipe etc.
To move in such a way as to create a whistling sound.

wholesale.en: The bombing resulted in wholesale destruction.

wicked.en: That was a wicked guitar solo, bro!
The band we went to see the other night was wicked loud!
The fabric wicks perspiration away from the body.
wicked.definition: To convey or draw off (liquid) by capillary action.

wicked.en: The moisture slowly wicked through the wood.
wicked.definition: (of a liquid) To traverse (i.e. be conveyed by capillary action) through a wick or other porous material, as water through a sponge. Usually followed by through.

worship.en: We worship at the church down the road.

worthwhile.en: Doing volunteer work to help others is truly worthwhile.
worthwhile.definition: Good and important enough to spend time, effort, or money on.

wreck.en: He was an emotional wreck after the death of his wife.
wreck.definition: Something or someone that has been ruined.

wreck.en: He wrecked the car in a collision.
wreck.definition: To destroy violently; to cause severe damage to something, to a point where it no longer works, or is useless.

zeal.en: She extols the virtues of veganism with missionary zeal.
zeal.definition: The fervour or tireless devotion for a person, cause, or ideal and determination in its furtherance; diligent enthusiasm; powerful interest.

zenith.en: Winning the continental championship was the zenith of my career.
zenith.definition: (by extension) Highest point or state; peak.
